```{r setup}
library(tidyverse) 
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(gt)
library(here)
library(patchwork)
library(marginaleffects)
library(ggdist)
library(ggtext)
library(tidybayes)
library(brms)
library(posterior)
library(ggh4x)
library(ggstats)

source(here::here("S6_Synthesis_model/functions/helper_functions_S6.R")) # Load helper functions specific to synthesis models
source(here::here("S6_Synthesis_model/functions/helper_functions_plot_extended.R")) # Load helper functions for extended plotting

```

## Framework sensitivity

```{r beta-framework}
#| include: false
# your code comparing PA vs abundance frameworks

#' List all files (using full paths)
beta_list <- list.files(
  "S2_get_beta_diversity/betadiv_output",
  recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE
)

# First, load every file into a tibble and extract metadata
df_loaded <- tibble(file = beta_list) |>
  mutate(
    # Extract dataset name from the file basename
    dataset = basename(file) |> str_remove("_beta_Output\\.rds"),
    # Get the directory part to extract more info (e.g., "Baselga_abun", "Podani_pa", etc.)
    dir_info = dirname(file) |> basename(),
    # Extract framework: assume it is either "Baselga" or "Podani"
    framework = str_extract(dir_info, "Baselga|Podani"),
    # Extract feature: assume it is either "abun" or "pa"
    feature = str_extract(dir_info, "abun|pa"),
    # Load the RDS object; each object is assumed to be a list with facets ("taxonomic", "functional")
    obj = map(file, readRDS)
  )

# Now, unnest the facets from each object.
# We assume each loaded object is a list where names correspond to facets.
df_tidy <- df_loaded |>
  mutate(
    facet_data = map(obj, ~ enframe(.x, name = "facet", value = "beta"))
  ) |>
  unnest(facet_data) |>
  select(dataset, feature, framework, facet, beta)

# At this point, each row is a combination of dataset, feature, framework, and facet,
# and 'beta' is the loaded dissimilarity object.

# If for each dataset/feature/facet you have separate rows for Podani and Baselga,
# you can then pivot wider to have one row per dataset/feature/facet with two columns.

df_wide <- df_tidy |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = framework,
    values_from = beta
  ) |>
  arrange(dataset, facet, feature) |> 
  mutate(
    Baselga = map(Baselga, ~ .x[[2]]),
    Podani  = map(Podani,  ~ .x[[2]])
  )  |> 
  rowwise() |> 
  mutate(cor = cor(Baselga,Podani)) |> 
  mutate(feature = ifelse(feature == "pa","Presence-absence","Abundance")) |> 
  mutate(facet = ifelse(facet == "Taxonomic","Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover")) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!dataset %in% c("N67TTP", "N78TTP", "S47TTP"))

# Compute summary statistics per group
df_summary <- df_wide |>
  mutate(facet = factor(facet, levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover"))) |> 
    mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels=c("Presence-absence","Abundance"))) |> 
  group_by(facet, feature) |>
  summarise(
    mean_cor = median(cor, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower_ci = quantile(cor, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper_ci = quantile(cor, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    label = paste0(" Median = ", format(round(mean_cor, 3),digits=3),
                   "\n95% CI [", format(round(lower_ci, 3),digits=3),
                   ", ", format(round(upper_ci, 3),digits=3), "]")
  ) 
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-corr-dist
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-cap: "**Distribution of Pearson correlations between abundance- and presence–absence-based $\\beta$-diversity estimates across datasets under the Podani and Baselga frameworks.** Histograms show the distribution of correlation coefficients for species and Functional turnover (n = 161 datasets). Each panel reports the median correlation and 95\\% credible interval (CI) across datasets, illustrating the overall correspondence between $\\beta$-diversity values derived from abundance and presence–absence data."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
#| out-width: 13cm
#| fig-pos: H

df_wide |> 
  select(cor,facet,feature) |> 
  drop_na(cor) |> #16 datasets did not produce beta diversity values in all frameworks and features
    mutate(facet = factor(facet, levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover"))) |> 
      mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels=c("Presence-absence","Abundance"))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = cor)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  facet_grid(facet ~ feature, scales="free") +
  geom_text(
    data = df_summary,
    mapping = aes(x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = label),
    hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1.1,
    size=convert_size(10)
  )+
  theme_bw()+
  labs(x="Pearson correlation", y = "Number of datasets")+
  xlim(0,NA)
```

```{r}
metadata<- readxl::read_excel("S6_Synthesis_model/data/synthesis_data.xlsx") |> 
  dplyr::filter(!dataset %in% c("N67TTP", "N78TTP", "S47TTP")) |> 
  rename(framework = "beta_type",
         feature = "metric_type")

r2_comparison <- metadata |> 
  select(dataset,facet,direction,harrel_davis,median_diff,framework,facet,feature,)

magnitude_hh_comparison <- metadata |> 
  select(dataset,facet,direction,framework,facet,feature,het_magnitude,direction)

magnitude_hfp_comparison <- metadata |> 
  select(dataset,facet,direction,framework,facet,feature,hfp_magnitude,direction)

shape_hh_comparison <- metadata |> 
  select(dataset,facet,direction,framework,facet,feature,het_modal_shape)

shape_hfp_comparison <- metadata |> 
  select(dataset,facet,direction,framework,facet,feature,hfp_modal_shape)
```

```{r}
direction_table <- r2_comparison |> 
  count(framework, facet, feature, direction) |> 
  arrange(framework, facet, feature, direction)

# Step 1: Recode for prettier labels
direction_table_clean <- direction_table |>
  mutate(
    facet = recode(facet,
                   "Taxonomic" = "Taxonomic turnover",
                   "Functional" = "Functional turnover"
    ),
    feature = recode(feature,
                     "pa" = "Presence-absence",
                     "abun" = "Abundance"
    )
  ) |> 
      mutate(facet = factor(facet, levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover"))) |> 
      mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels=c("Presence-absence","Abundance")))

# Step 2: Pivot to wide format
direction_nested <- direction_table_clean |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = direction, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>
  arrange(feature, facet, framework)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-t1
#| tbl-cap: "Number of datasets showing compositional *differentiation* or *homogenisation* 
#|           for species and Functional turnover by dissimilarity framework (Baselga and Podani) and data type (abundance and presence-absence)"
#| tbl-pos: H

table_direction <- direction_nested |>
  mutate(feature = gsub("–", "-", feature)) |>
  gt(groupname_col = "feature") |>
  cols_label(
    framework = "Feature",
    facet = "Facet",
    differentiation = "Increase",
    homogenisation = "Decrease"
  ) |>
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Relationship direction",
    columns = c(differentiation, homogenisation)
  ) |>
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(differentiation, homogenisation),
    decimals = 0
  ) |>
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(11),
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold",
    row_group.font.weight = "bold",
    heading.title.font.size = 14,
    heading.subtitle.font.size = 12
  )

table_direction
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-quantile-corr
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5
#| out-width: 13cm
#| out-height: 10cm
#| fig-cap: "**Correlation between Harrell–Davis quantile estimates across dissimilarity frameworks and data types.** Pairwise correlations between $\\Delta r^2$ estimates derived from the Podani and Baselga frameworks for species and Functional turnover. Each point represents one dataset (n = 161). Panels distinguish abundance- and presence–absence-based data. Pearson correlation coefficients and their 95\\% credible intervals (CI) are reported within each panel."
#| fig-pos: H

# Recode levels for clearer facet labels
r2_wide <- r2_comparison |> 
  select(dataset, facet, feature, framework, harrel_davis) |> 
  pivot_wider(names_from = framework, values_from = harrel_davis) |> 
  drop_na(Baselga, Podani) |> 
  mutate(
    feature = recode(feature, pa = "Presence-absence", abun = "Abundance"),
    facet = recode(facet, Taxonomic = "Taxonomic turnover", Functional = "Functional turnover")
  ) |> 
        mutate(facet = factor(facet, levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover"))) |> 
      mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels=c("Presence-absence","Abundance")))

                                                                  
cor_annot <- r2_wide |> 
  group_by(facet, feature) |> 
  summarise(
    cor_test = list(cor.test(Baselga, Podani, method = "pearson")),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    r = map_dbl(cor_test, ~ .x$estimate),
    lwr = map_dbl(cor_test, ~ .x$conf.int[1]),
    upr = map_dbl(cor_test, ~ .x$conf.int[2]),
    label = paste0(
      "r = ", round(r, 2),
      "\n95% CI [", round(lwr, 2), ", ", round(upr, 2), "]"
    )
  )

# Plot
ggplot(r2_wide, aes(x = Baselga, y = Podani)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  geom_text(
    data = cor_annot,
    aes(x = -50, y = 1, label = label),  # adjust position as needed
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3,
    fontface = "bold",
    hjust=0
  ) +
labs(
  x = "Baselga (directional support; standardised \u0394R\u00b2)",
  y = "Podani (directional support; standardised \u0394R\u00b2)"
)+
  facet_grid(feature ~ facet) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family="sans") +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-metric-corr
#| tbl-cap: "Correlations among alternative directional effect size metrics for species and trait turnover."
#| tbl-pos: H

df_clean <- data.frame(
  Facet = c(rep("Taxonomic turnover", 3),
            rep("Functional turnover", 3)),
  Metric1 = c("Robust median ΔR²_std",
              "Robust median ΔR²_std",
              "Overlap-adjusted median ΔR²",
              "Robust median ΔR²_std",
              "Robust median ΔR²_std",
              "Overlap-adjusted median ΔR²"),
  Metric2 = c("Overlap-adjusted median ΔR²",
              "Weighted median ΔR²_std",
              "Weighted median ΔR²_std",
              "Overlap-adjusted median ΔR²",
              "Weighted median ΔR²_std",
              "Weighted median ΔR²_std"),
  r  = c(0.980, 0.834, 0.855, 0.966, 0.818, 0.811),
  t  = c(61.40, 19.08, 20.82, 46.94, 17.95, 17.45),
  df = rep(159, 6),
  p  = c(1.3e-87, 5.4e-47, 2.7e-52, 2.6e-74, 2.8e-38, 7.1e-38)
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    Facet = factor(
      Facet,
      levels = c("Taxonomic turnover", "Functional turnover")
    )
  )


gt(df_clean, groupname_col = "Facet") |>
  cols_label(
    Metric1 = "Metric 1",
    Metric2 = "Metric 2",
    r  = gt::html("$r$"),
    t  = gt::html("$t$"),
    df = gt::html("df"),
    p  = gt::html("$p$")
  ) |>
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Correlation statistics",
    columns = c(r, t, df, p)
  ) |>
  fmt_scientific(columns = p, decimals = 2) |>
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(11),
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold",
    row_group.font.weight = "bold",
    heading.title.font.size = 14,
    heading.subtitle.font.size = 12
  ) 


```
```{r}
#| include: false

# Recode levels for clearer facet labels
ef_hfp_wide <- magnitude_hfp_comparison |> 
  select(dataset, facet, feature, framework, hfp_magnitude) |> 
  pivot_wider(names_from = framework, values_from = hfp_magnitude) |> 
  drop_na(Baselga, Podani) |> 
  mutate(
    feature = recode(feature, pa = "Presence-absence", abun = "Abundance"),
    facet = recode(facet, Taxonomic = "Taxonomic turnover", Functional = "Functional turnover")
  )

# Set facet order manually
ef_hfp_wide$facet <- factor(ef_hfp_wide$facet, levels = c("Taxonomic turnover", "Functional turnover"))
ef_hfp_wide$feature <- factor(ef_hfp_wide$feature, levels = c("Presence-absence", "Abundance"))

```

```{r}
#| label: "tbl-hfp-wilcoxon"
#| tbl-cap: "Comparison of Podani vs Baselga Human Footprint effect magnitudes"
#| table-pos: H

# 1) Compute paired Wilcoxon summaries
summary_tbl <- ef_hfp_wide |>
  mutate(feature = gsub("–", "-", feature)) |> 
  group_by(facet, feature) |>
  summarise(
    wilcox_p   = wilcox.test(Baselga, Podani, paired = TRUE)$p.value,
    median_diff = median(Podani - Baselga),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    # format p-values: show "<0.001" when very small
    wilcox_p   = ifelse(wilcox_p < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", wilcox_p)),
    # format median differences to three decimals
    median_diff = sprintf("%.3f", median_diff)
  )

# 2) Render with gt
summary_tbl |>
  gt(
    rowname_col   = "feature",
    groupname_col = "facet"
  ) |>
  cols_label(
    facet       = "Replacement facet",
    feature     = "Data type",
    wilcox_p    = "Wilcoxon p-value",
    median_diff = "Median diff. (Podani – Baselga)"
  ) |>
  sub_missing(
    columns     = everything(),
    missing_text = "-"
  ) |>
  cols_align(
    align   = "center",
    columns = c(wilcox_p, median_diff)
  ) |>
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(12),
    heading.title.font.size = px(14)
  ) 
```

```{r}
#| label: "tbl-hh-wilcoxon"
#| tbl-cap: "Comparison of Podani vs Baselga Habitat Heterogeneity effect magnitudes"
#| table-pos: H

# 1) Compute paired Wilcoxon summaries
summary_tbl_hh <- ef_hh_wide |>
    mutate(feature = gsub("–", "-", feature)) |> 
  group_by(facet, feature) |>
  summarise(
    wilcox_p   = wilcox.test(Baselga, Podani, paired = TRUE)$p.value,
    median_diff = median(Podani - Baselga),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    # format p-values: show "<0.001" when very small
    wilcox_p   = ifelse(wilcox_p < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", wilcox_p)),
    # format median differences to three decimals
    median_diff = sprintf("%.3f", median_diff)
  )

# 2) Render with gt
summary_tbl_hh |>
  gt(
    rowname_col   = "feature",
    groupname_col = "facet"
  ) |>
  cols_label(
    facet       = "Replacement facet",
    feature     = "Data type",
    wilcox_p    = "Wilcoxon p-value",
    median_diff = "Median diff. (Podani – Baselga)"
  ) |>
  fmt_missing(
    columns     = everything(),
    missing_text = "-"
  ) |>
  cols_align(
    align   = "center",
    columns = c(wilcox_p, median_diff)
  ) |>
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(12),
#    group.label.font.weight = "bold",
    heading.title.font.size = px(14)
  ) 
```

```{r magnitude-direction-hfp, echo=FALSE}

direction_colors = c("Dissimilarity" = "#ff7f32","Similarity" = "#9f5cc0")  

# 1. Summarise medians and 95% CIs
plot_df_hfp <- magnitude_hfp_comparison |>
  mutate(
    facet     = recode(facet, Functional = "Functional turnover", Taxonomic = "Taxonomic turnover"),
    feature   = recode(feature, pa = "Presence-absence", abun = "Abundance"),
    framework = factor(framework, c("Baselga", "Podani")),
    direction = factor(direction, c("differentiation", "homogenisation"),
                       labels = c("Dissimilarity", "Similarity"))
  ) |>
  group_by(facet, feature, framework, direction) |>
  summarise(
    median = Hmisc::smean.cl.boot(hfp_magnitude)[1],
    lower  = Hmisc::smean.cl.boot(hfp_magnitude)[2],
    upper  = Hmisc::smean.cl.boot(hfp_magnitude)[3],
    label  = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f–%.2f)", median, lower, upper),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  mutate(facet = factor(facet, levels = c("Taxonomic turnover", "Functional turnover"))) |> 
   mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels=c("Presence-absence","Abundance")))
  
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-hfp-magnitude
#| fig-cap: "**Magnitude of human footprint effects on species and trait turnover across dissimilarity frameworks and data types**. Effect magnitudes were quantified as the sum of fitted I-spline coefficients from the generalized dissimilarity models (GDMs) describing the response of $\\beta$-diversity to human footprint intensity. Results are shown separately for communities classified as homogenising or differentiating, across combinations of biodiversity facet (species vs traits), data type (abundance vs presence–absence), and dissimilarity partitioning framework (Baselga vs Podani). Points represent bootstrap medians and horizontal bars the 95% confidence limits (n = 1000). Across all combinations, effect magnitudes are consistently higher for homogenising communities, indicating that human-driven turnover intensifies when it leads to functional or compositional convergence."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 15cm

ggplot(plot_df_hfp, aes(y = direction, x = median, color = direction)) +
  geom_pointrange(
    aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper),
    position = position_dodge(width = .7),
    size = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = label),
    colour="black",
    position = position_dodge(width = .3),
    vjust = -1.2,
    hjust=0,
    size = 3
  ) +
  ggh4x::facet_nested(framework+feature ~ facet, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    y = NULL,
    x = "Cumulative fitted turnover (along Human Footprint)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )+
  scale_colour_manual(values=direction_colors)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-hh-magnitude
#| fig-cap: "**Magnitude of habitat heterogeneity effects on species and trait turnover across dissimilarity frameworks and data types**. Effect magnitudes were quantified as the sum of fitted I-spline coefficients from the generalized dissimilarity models (GDMs) describing the response of β-diversity to habitat heterogeneity. Results are shown separately for communities classified as homogenising or differentiating, across combinations of biodiversity facet (species vs traits), data type (abundance vs presence–absence), and dissimilarity partitioning framework (Baselga vs Podani). Points represent bootstrap medians and horizontal bars the 95% confidence limits (n = 1000). Across all combinations, effect magnitudes are consistently higher for homogenising communities, indicating that trait and compositional turnover intensify under human-driven convergence."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 15cm
# 2. Plot
ggplot(plot_df_hh, aes(y = direction, x = median, color = direction)) +
  geom_pointrange(
    aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper),
    position = position_dodge(width = .7),
    size = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = label),
    colour="black",
    position = position_dodge(width = .3),
    vjust = -1.2,
    hjust=0.2,
    size = 3
  ) +
  ggh4x::facet_nested(framework+feature ~ facet, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    y = NULL,
    x = "Cumulative fitted turnover (along Habitat Heterogeneity)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )+
  scale_colour_manual(values=direction_colors) 
```

### Shape or relationship

Across all combinations of frameworks, data types, and biodiversity facets, the shape of community responses to both human footprint and habitat heterogeneity was overwhelmingly consistent. Saturating relationships dominated, followed by exponential curves, whereas reverse-logistic and absent responses occurred only rarely. This pattern was stable across species and trait turnover, and across both Baselga and Podani partitioning schemes, indicating that the overall functional form of $\beta$-diversity responses is largely insensitive to the choice of dissimilarity framework or data weighting. Concordance between frameworks was particularly strong—off-diagonal mismatches, where Baselga and Podani assigned different shapes to the same dataset, represented fewer than $10,%$ of all cases. These results demonstrate that most communities exhibit a rapid initial phase of compositional or functional change at low levels of environmental pressure, followed by a plateau, highlighting the generality of early, saturating turnover responses across biodiversity dimensions and data representations (@fig-hfp-shape; @fig-hh-shape).

```{r}
#| label: fig-hfp-shape
#| fig-cap: "**Concordance in response-shape classifications across dissimilarity frameworks, data types, and biodiversity facets**. Heatmaps show the number of datasets assigned to each combination of shape categories (absent, exponential, reverse-logistic, saturating) under the Baselga (columns) and Podani (rows) frameworks for the effect of *human footprint* on community turnover. Results are presented separately for species and Functional turnover, each split by abundance- and presence–absence-based data. Cell intensity indicates the frequency of each cross-classification. Strong diagonals denote high agreement in shape detection between frameworks, whereas off-diagonal counts reflect datasets where the inferred response shape differs. Across both facets and data types, most datasets align along the diagonal, confirming that the functional form of $\\beta$-diversity responses to human pressure is consistent across methodological choices."
#| fig-pos: H

# 1. Prepare cross-tab counts (ignore direction)
cross_hfp <- shape_hfp_comparison |>
  select(dataset, facet, framework, feature, hfp_modal_shape) |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(
    facet   = recode(facet, Functional = "Functional turnover", Taxonomic = "Taxonomic turnover"),
    feature = recode(feature, pa = "Presence-absence", abun = "Abundance")
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = c(dataset, facet, feature),
    names_from  = framework,
    values_from = hfp_modal_shape
  ) |>
  group_by(facet, feature) |>
  count(Baselga, Podani) |>
  ungroup() |> 
      mutate(facet = factor(facet, levels = c("Taxonomic turnover", "Functional turnover"))) |> 
   mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels=c("Presence-absence","Abundance")))

# Plot heatmap with counts and white‐to‐blue fill
ggplot(cross_hfp, aes(x = Baselga, y = Podani, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), size = 3) +
  facet_grid(feature ~ facet) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low  = "white",
    high = "blue",
    name = "Count"
  ) +
  labs(
    x     = "Baselga framework",
    y     = "Podani framework",
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid       = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-hh-shape
#| fig-cap: "**Concordance in response-shape classifications across dissimilarity frameworks, data types, and biodiversity facets**. Heatmaps show the number of datasets assigned to each combination of shape categories (absent, exponential, reverse-logistic, saturating) under the Baselga (columns) and Podani (rows) frameworks for the effect of *habitat heterogeneity* on community turnover. Results are presented separately for species and Functional turnover, each split by abundance- and presence–absence-based data. Cell intensity indicates the frequency of each cross-classification. Strong diagonals denote high agreement in shape detection between frameworks, whereas off-diagonal counts reflect datasets where the inferred response shape differs. Across both facets and data types, most datasets align along the diagonal, confirming that the functional form of $\\beta$-diversity responses to environmental heterogeneity is consistent across methodological choices."
#| fig-pos: H


# 1. Prepare cross-tab counts (ignore direction)
cross_het <- shape_hh_comparison |>
  select(dataset, facet, framework, feature, het_modal_shape) |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(
    facet   = recode(facet, Functional = "Functional turnover", Taxonomic = "Taxonomic turnover"),
    feature = recode(feature, pa = "Presence-absence", abun = "Abundance")
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = c(dataset, facet, feature),
    names_from  = framework,
    values_from = het_modal_shape
  ) |>
  group_by(facet, feature) |>
  count(Baselga, Podani) |>
  ungroup() |> 
      mutate(facet = factor(facet, levels = c("Taxonomic turnover", "Functional turnover"))) |> 
   mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels=c("Presence-absence","Abundance")))

# Plot heatmap with counts and white‐to‐blue fill
ggplot(cross_het, aes(x = Baselga, y = Podani, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), size = 3) +
  facet_grid(feature ~ facet) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low  = "white",
    high = "blue",
    name = "Count"
  ) +
  labs(
    x     = "Baselga framework",
    y     = "Podani framework",
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid       = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-raw-diss
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 7
#| out-width: 13cm
#| fig-cap: "**Mean and range of dissimilarity values across datasets for species and trait turnover**. Each point represents a dataset’s mean dissimilarity with $95\\%$ confidence limits under the Baselga and Podani frameworks, shown separately for abundance- and presence-absence-based data. Species turnover spans broader dissimilarity ranges than trait turnover, confirming that taxonomic composition varies more strongly across sites than functional attributes."
#| fig-pos: H
#| 
# Process the nested df_wide object to extract summary statistics for each beta framework
df_summary <- df_wide |>
  ungroup() |> 
  # Convert the dist objects to numeric vectors
  mutate(
    Baselga_values = map(Baselga, ~ as.numeric(.x)),
    Podani_values = map(Podani, ~ as.numeric(.x)),
  ) |>
  # Pivot the two columns into a long format
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Baselga_values", "Podani_values"),
               names_to = "beta_type", values_to = "values") |>
  # Clean up beta_type names (remove _values suffix)
  mutate(beta_type = sub("_values", "", beta_type)) |>
  # Calculate median and quantiles for each row
  mutate(
    median = map_dbl(values, ~ Hmisc::smedian.hilow(.x, na.rm = TRUE)[1]),
    lower  = map_dbl(values, ~ Hmisc::smedian.hilow(.x, na.rm = TRUE)[2]),
    upper  = map_dbl(values, ~ Hmisc::smedian.hilow(.x, na.rm = TRUE)[3])
  ) |> 
  select(dataset,feature,facet,median,lower,upper,beta_type) 

make_plot <- function(data, framework_val, feature_val, dodge_width = 0.5) {
  
  # 1. Filter to the desired combination
  d <- data |>
    filter(beta_type == framework_val, feature == feature_val)
  
  # 2. Compute ordering based on species facet
  order_df <- d |>
    filter(facet == "Taxonomic turnover") |>
    mutate(order_val = as.numeric(fct_reorder(dataset, median, .desc = FALSE))) |>
    select(dataset, order_val)
  
  d <- d |>
    left_join(order_df, by = "dataset") |>
    mutate(dataset_order_local = fct_reorder(dataset, order_val, .desc = FALSE)) |>
    ungroup()
  
  # 3. Compute summary for both facets
  stats <- d |>
    group_by(facet) |>
    summarise(
      mean_med = mean(median),
      lower_ci = mean(lower),
      upper_ci = mean(upper),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  # assemble single label text
  label_text <- paste0(
    "Species = ", sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]",
                          stats$mean_med[stats$facet=="Taxonomic turnover"],
                          stats$lower_ci[stats$facet=="Taxonomic turnover"],
                          stats$upper_ci[stats$facet=="Taxonomic turnover"]
    ),
    "\nTraits  = ", sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]",
                            stats$mean_med[stats$facet=="Functional turnover"],
                            stats$lower_ci[stats$facet=="Functional turnover"],
                            stats$upper_ci[stats$facet=="Functional turnover"]
    )
  )
  
  pos_dodge <- position_dodge(width = dodge_width)
  
  # 4. Build the plot with geom_label
  ggplot(d, aes(x = dataset_order_local, y = median)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper, color = facet),
                  width = 0.1, position = pos_dodge, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_point(position = pos_dodge, shape = 21, aes(fill = facet)) +
    
    # white‐filled label so text is readable
    geom_label(
      x = 25,
      y = 0.5, 
      label = label_text,
      fill     = "white",
      label.size = .2,
      hjust    = 0,
      vjust    = 0.5,
      size     = convert_size(8),
      fontface = "bold",
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    
    coord_flip() +
    labs(x     = "Dataset",
         y     = "Dissimilarity (median ± 95% CI)",
         title = paste(framework_val, "—", feature_val),
         color = "Facet",
         fill  = "Facet") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      axis.text.y     = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid= element_blank(),
    ) +
scale_colour_manual(
  breaks = c("Taxonomic turnover", "Functional turnover"),
  values = c("Taxonomic turnover" = "#0072B2",
             "Functional turnover" = "#D55E00")
) +
scale_fill_manual(
  breaks = c("Taxonomic turnover", "Functional turnover"),
  values = c("Taxonomic turnover" = "#0072B2",
             "Functional turnover" = "#D55E00")
)
    
}


# Create the four separate plots:
p1 <- make_plot(df_summary, "Baselga", "Presence-absence")
p2 <- make_plot(df_summary, "Baselga", "Abundance")
p3 <- make_plot(df_summary, "Podani",  "Presence-absence") 
p4 <- make_plot(df_summary, "Podani",  "Abundance")

p1+p2+p3+p4 + plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = "bottom")

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-hfp-mag-mean-diss
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5
#| out-width: 13cm
#| out-height: 10cm
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-cap: "**Relationship between mean dissimilarity and the magnitude of human footprint effects on community turnover**. Panels show species and Functional turnover under both Baselga and Podani frameworks for abundance- and presence–absence-based data. Red lines indicate fitted trends with $95\\%$ confidence bands. Differentiation effects increase with mean dissimilarity for species, while trait-based and homogenisation responses show no consistent association."
#| fig-pos: H


magnitude_by_range <- df_summary |>
  select(dataset, feature, facet, beta_type, median, lower, upper) |>
  rename(framework = beta_type) |>
  left_join(
    metadata |>
      select(dataset, feature, facet, framework,
             hfp_magnitude, het_magnitude, direction) |>
      mutate(
        feature = ifelse(feature == "pa", "Presence-absence", "Abundance"),
        facet   = ifelse(facet == "Taxonomic",
                         "Taxonomic turnover",
                         "Functional turnover"),
        direction = ifelse(direction == "differentiation",
                           "Increasing dissimilarity",
                           "Increasing similarity")
      )
  ) |>
  mutate(
    facet   = factor(facet,
                     levels = c("Taxonomic turnover",
                                "Functional turnover")),
    feature = factor(feature,
                     levels = c("Presence-absence","Abundance")),
    direction = factor(direction,
                       levels = c("Increasing dissimilarity",
                                  "Increasing similarity"))
  )

magnitude_by_range |>
  drop_na() |>
  ggplot(aes(x = hfp_magnitude + 0.001, y = median)) +

  # uncertainty (lighter & cleaner)
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
                 alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.3) +

  # points
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "grey30",
             size = 1.2, stroke = 0) +

  # trend
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              colour = "red",
              linewidth = 0.6,
              se = TRUE,
              alpha = 0.15) +

  scale_x_log10() +

  labs(
    x = expression(Log[10]~"Cumulative turnover response to Human Footprint"),
    y = expression("Observed turnover range (median ± 95% CI)")
  ) +

  ggh4x::facet_nested(
    feature + framework ~ facet + direction,
    scales = "free_y",
    independent = "y"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", colour = NA),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

## Direction of turnover

### Podani

#### Abundance

##### Contrasts (Main land use type)

```{r}
#| label: fig-pod-abun-contr-dir
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5
#| out-width: 13cm
#| out-height: 10cm
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-cap: "**Pairwise contrasts in the direction of community turnover among land-use types.** Posterior contrasts for the direction of community turnover (Harrell–Davis $\\Delta r^{2}$) across land-use categories following the Podani framework using abundance data. Positive values indicate a stronger signal of homogenisation in the first land-use type relative to the second. Points represent posterior medians and horizontal bars show 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% credible intervals."
#| fig-pos: H

direction_species_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Abundance/direction/direction_species_base.rds"))
direction_traits_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Abundance/direction/direction_traits_base.rds"))

#pairwise contrasts 

species_contrast_pod_abun<- avg_comparisons(
  direction_species_pod_abun,
  variables = list(main_land_use_type = "pairwise"),
  re_formula = NA,
  type       = "response",
  conf_level = .95
)  |> 
  posterior_draws() |> 
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(estimate,2)} [{round(conf.low,2)},{round(conf.high,2)}]")) |> 
  separate_wider_delim(contrast,delim="-",names=c("var1","var2")) |> 
  mutate(across(var1:var2, ~ gsub("^\\s*mean\\((.*?)\\)\\s*$", "\\1", .x))) |>
  mutate(facet="Taxonomic turnover") |> 
  mutate(comparison = paste0(var1,"-",var2))  |> 
  mutate(comparison = fct_reorder(comparison,estimate)) |> 
  arrange(comparison)

trait_contrast_pod_abun<- avg_comparisons(
  direction_traits_pod_abun,
  variables = list(main_land_use_type = "pairwise"),
  re_formula = NA,
  type       = "response",
  conf_level = .95
)  |> 
  posterior_draws() |> 
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(estimate,2)} [{round(conf.low,2)},{round(conf.high,2)}]")) |> 
  separate_wider_delim(contrast,delim="-",names=c("var1","var2")) |> 
  mutate(across(var1:var2, ~ gsub("^\\s*mean\\((.*?)\\)\\s*$", "\\1", .x))) |>
  mutate(facet="Functional turnover") |> 
  mutate(comparison = paste0(var1,"-",var2))  |> 
  mutate(comparison = fct_reorder(comparison,estimate)) |> 
  arrange(comparison)

Figure_10a<-
  species_contrast_pod_abun |> 
  ggplot(aes(y=comparison,x=draw))+
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  stat_pointinterval(.width=c(.8,.89,.95))+
  facet_wrap(~facet, ncol=1) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = distinct(species_contrast_pod_abun, comparison,.keep_all=TRUE) |>  pull(var1)) +
  geom_text(data=~.x |> distinct(comparison,.keep_all=TRUE),
            aes(y=comparison,x=conf.high, label=label),hjust=-.1)+
  geom_text(data=~.x |> distinct(comparison,.keep_all=TRUE),
            aes(y=comparison, label=var2),x=12,hjust=0, colour = "gray30", size=convert_size(12))+
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(-2,0,2,4,6,8,10))+
   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(NA, 15), clip = 'off') +
    guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )+
  labs(x="Posterior draws",y="")
  
# Create the plot

Figure_10b <- trait_contrast_pod_abun |> 
  ggplot(aes(y=comparison,x=draw))+
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  stat_pointinterval(.width=c(.8,.89,.95))+
  facet_wrap(~facet, ncol=1) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = distinct(trait_contrast_pod_abun, comparison,.keep_all=TRUE) |>  pull(var1)) +
  geom_text(data=~.x |> distinct(comparison,.keep_all=TRUE),
            aes(y=comparison,x=conf.high, label=label),hjust=-.1)+
  geom_text(data=~.x |> distinct(comparison,.keep_all=TRUE),
            aes(y=comparison, label=var2),x=12,hjust=0, colour = "gray30", size=convert_size(12))+
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(-2,0,2,4,6,8,10))+
   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(NA, 15), clip = 'off') +
    guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )+
  labs(x="Posterior draws",y="")
  
Figure_10a/Figure_10b
```

##### Random effect estimates

```{r}
#| label: fig-podani-abun-dir-rf
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5
#| out-width: 13cm
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-cap: "**Taxon-level deviations in the direction of community turnover.** Random-effect estimates from the direction models (Harrell–Davis $\\Delta r^{2}$) showing taxon-specific deviations from the overall model intercept for species and Functional turnover following Podani framework using abundance data. Positive values indicate stronger homogenisation signals relative to the global mean, while negative values reflect stronger differentiation. Points denote posterior medians and horizontal bars represent 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% credible intervals."
#| fig-pos: H

# Prepare and summarize data
direction_species_re_pod_abun <- direction_species_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(r_taxa_coarse[taxa_coarse, Intercept]) |>
  group_by(taxa_coarse) |>
  median_qi(r_taxa_coarse, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(r_taxa_coarse, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate")))

direction_traits_re_pod_abun <- direction_traits_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(r_taxa_coarse[taxa_coarse, Intercept]) |>
  group_by(taxa_coarse) |>
  median_qi(r_taxa_coarse, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(r_taxa_coarse, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]"))  |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate")))

# Plot
Figure_11a <- direction_species_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(r_taxa_coarse[taxa_coarse, Intercept]) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet="Taxonomic turnover") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = r_taxa_coarse, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = direction_species_re_pod_abun,
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_wrap(~facet)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-5, 5), clip = "on") +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  ) 


Figure_11b <- direction_traits_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(r_taxa_coarse[taxa_coarse, Intercept]) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet="Functional turnover") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = r_taxa_coarse, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = direction_traits_re_pod_abun |> mutate(taxa_coarse =   str_to_title(taxa_coarse)),
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_wrap(~facet)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-5, 5), clip = "on") +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  ) 

Figure_11a / Figure_11b
```

#### Presence-absence

##### Posterior predictions

```{r}
#| label: Podani_PA_posterior_pred
#| include: false
direction_species_pod_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Presence_absence/direction/direction_species_base.rds"))
direction_traits_pod_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Presence_absence/direction/direction_traits_base.rds"))

best_model_species <- direction_species_pod_pa
best_model_traits <- direction_traits_pod_pa

model_data<- readxl::read_excel("S6_Synthesis_model/data/synthesis_data.xlsx") |> 
  dplyr::filter(!dataset %in% c("N67TTP", "N78TTP", "S47TTP")) |> 
  rename(framework = "beta_type",
         feature = "metric_type") |> 
  filter(framework == "Podani", feature == "pa") |> 
  mutate(facet = ifelse(facet == "Taxonomic","Species","Traits")) |> 
  rename(direction_harrel_davis = harrel_davis)

species_draws_eco <- predictions(
  best_model_species,
  newdata = datagrid(ecosystem_type=unique),
  re_formula = NA,
  type = "response"
) |>
  posterior_draws() |>
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover")

trait_draws_eco <- predictions(
  best_model_traits,
  newdata = datagrid(ecosystem_type=unique),
  re_formula = NA,
  type = "response"
) |>
  posterior_draws() |>
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")

pred_eco_type <- bind_rows(species_draws_eco, trait_draws_eco) |>
  select(draw, ecosystem_type, facet) |>
  mutate(
    facet = factor(facet,
                   levels = c("Taxonomic turnover",
                              "Functional turnover")),
    ecosystem_type = factor(ecosystem_type, levels = c("Freshwater","Terrestrial"))
  )|> 
  mutate(level="Ecosystem type")


#Land use values 


species_draws_lu <- predictions(
  best_model_species,
  newdata = datagrid(main_land_use_type=unique),
  re_formula = NA,
  type = "response"
) |>
  posterior_draws() |>
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover")

trait_draws_lu <- predictions(
  best_model_traits,
  newdata = datagrid(main_land_use_type=unique),
  re_formula = NA,
  type = "response"
) |>
  posterior_draws() |>
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")

pred_main_lu <- bind_rows(species_draws_lu, trait_draws_lu) |>
  select(draw, main_land_use_type, facet) |>
  mutate(
    facet = factor(facet,
                   levels = c("Taxonomic turnover",
                              "Functional turnover")),
    main_land_use_type = factor(main_land_use_type, levels = rev(c("Agriculture", "Forest", "Urban", "Multiple")))
  ) |> 
  mutate(level="Main land use")


# Define colors outside the individual figures
blue_green <- c(Freshwater = "#118ab2", Terrestrial = "#06d6a0")
land_use_colors <- c(Agriculture = "#a36627", Forest = "#848c04", Urban = "#1c1c0c", Multiple = "#dc7c5c")


annotations_df <- data.frame(
  label = c(
    "<span>&larr; Dissimilarity</span>",
    "<span>Similarity &rarr;</span>",
    "<span>&larr; Dissimilarity</span>",
    "<span>Similarity &rarr;</span>"
  ),
  x = c(-0.03, 0.03, -7, -7),
  y = c(6.5, 6.5, -0.2, 0.2),
  angle = c(0, 0, 90, 90),
  hjust = c(1, 0, 1, 0)
)
```

```{r}
#| label: pod-pa-posterior-pred-fig 
#| fig-cap: "**Direction of community turnover across ecosystem and land-use types**. a, Dataset-level Harrell–Davis Δr² values for species and Functional turnover under the Podani framework with presence–absence data (n = 161 datasets). Positive values indicate stronger support for homogenisation, negative values indicate differentiation. b–c, Posterior predictions from multilevel models for Δr² values by ecosystem type (b) and dominant land use (c). Points show posterior medians; bars show 80%, 89%, and 95% highest-density intervals (HDIs). Intervals excluding zero indicate a consistent directional tendency."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

p1<-model_data |>
  select(dataset, facet, direction_harrel_davis) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = facet, values_from = direction_harrel_davis) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = Species, x = Traits)) +
  geom_richtext(
    data = annotations_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, angle = angle, hjust = hjust), 
    size=convert_size(12),
    label.color = NA,
    inherit.aes=FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = .7) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-7, NA), ylim = c(-10, 7),clip="on") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0,
             linetype = 3,
             linewidth = .1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
             linetype = 3,
             linewidth = .1) +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(
      face = "bold",
      margin = margin(b = 5)
    ),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(
      family = "sans",
      hjust = 0.5,
      margin = margin(t = 5)
    ),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(
      family = "sans",
      angle = 90,
      margin = margin(r = 4)
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(
      family = "sans",
      color = "grey30",
      margin = margin(t = 5)
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(
      family = "sans",
      color = "grey30",
    ),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  ) +
  labs(
    x   = "Standardised difference in model fit<br><br><b>Functional turnover</b>",
    y   = "<b>Taxonomic turnover</b><br><br>Standardised difference in model fit",
    tag = "a"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=c(-6.5, -4.5, -2.5, 0, 2.5))+
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )

annotations_p2 <- data.frame(
  label = c(
    "<span>&larr; Dissimilarity</span>",
    "<span>Similarity &rarr;</span>"
  ),
  x      = rep(c(-0.03,  0.03),2),   # tweak these to your actual x‐limits
  y      = c( 2.5,     2.5,2.5,2.5),   # y position above your points
  angle  = c( 0,     0,0,0   ),
  hjust  = c( 1,     0,1,0   ),
  facet  = c("Taxonomic turnover", "Taxonomic turnover", "Functional turnover","Functional turnover")  # or repeat for "Trait replacement"
) |>  mutate(facet = factor(facet,levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover")))


p2<- ggplot(pred_eco_type, aes(y = ecosystem_type, x = draw, colour = ecosystem_type)) +
  stat_pointinterval(.width = c(.8, .9, .95)) +
  geom_richtext(
    data = annotations_p2,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, angle = angle, hjust = hjust), 
    size=convert_size(10),
    label.color = NA,
    inherit.aes=FALSE
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 3) +
  facet_grid(~facet)+
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(blue_green, land_use_colors)) +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 14) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans", face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 12, margin=margin(t=2)),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans", hjust = 0.5, size = 12, margin=margin(t=2)),
    axis.text.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans", size = 12, margin=margin(t=5),hjust=1),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans", size = 12, margin=margin(t=5)),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth=0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth=0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.3, "lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(3, 0, 3, 0, unit="mm"),
    legend.position="none",
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14)
  )+
  scale_y_discrete(expand=c(.1,1), labels=c("Freshwater (n=30)","Terrestrial (n=131)"))+
  labs(x="",tag="b")+
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-3,3),clip="off")

p3<- ggplot(pred_main_lu, aes(y = main_land_use_type, x = draw, colour = main_land_use_type)) +
  stat_pointinterval(.width = c(.8, .9, .95)) +

    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
```
