#### Abundance

##### Random effect estimates

```{r}
#| label: fig-pod-abun-mag-rf
#| fig-cap: "**Taxon-level variation in the magnitude of community turnover under the Podani framework using abundance data.** Posterior estimates of random intercepts for major taxonomic groups (microorganisms, plants, invertebrates, vertebrates) from Bayesian models quantifying the magnitude of species and Functional turnover. Values represent deviations from the overall mean magnitude (sum of I-spline coefficients), indicating whether each taxon exhibits stronger or weaker turnover relative to the global average. Points show posterior medians, and intervals represent 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDIs)."
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-pos: H

magnitude_species_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Abundance/magnitude/magnitude_species_base.rds"))

magnitude_traits_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Abundance/magnitude/magnitude_trait_base.rds"))


# Prepare and summarize data

magnitude_species_re_pod_abun <-
magnitude_species_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
    mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover")

magnitude_traits_re_pod_abun <- 
  magnitude_traits_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
    mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")

# Plot
Figure_24a <- magnitude_species_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |> 
    mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = magnitude_species_re_pod_abun,
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  ) 


Figure_24b <- magnitude_traits_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet="Functional turnover") |> 
    mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = magnitude_traits_re_pod_abun |> mutate(taxa_coarse =   str_to_title(taxa_coarse)),
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  ) 

Figure_24a / Figure_24b
```

#### Presence-absence

##### Posterior predictions

```{r}
#| label: fig-pred-pod-pa-mag
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the magnitude of human-pressure effects on community turnover following the Podani framework using presence-absence data.** Posterior predictions of the magnitude of effect in each direction across ecosystem type (terrestrial, freshwater) and main land-use type (agriculture, forest, urban, multiple-use). For each group, the central dot is the median prediction, and the linewidth represent the 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals; an interval that does not overlap zero is interpreted as a significant tendency toward homogenisation or differentiation (see Methods). The x-axis indicates the weighted effect size, estimated by the sum of human pressure I-splines from BBGDM. Effect sizes were weighted by the proportion of valid bootstraps (target = 1000), penalising models with lower success rates. Dots represent median values, with lines showing 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest density intervals (HDI). Relationships were considered significant if the HDI did not include zero."
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 5.5
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

magnitude_species_pod_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Presence_absence/magnitude/magnitude_species_base.rds"))

magnitude_traits_pod_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Presence_absence/magnitude/magnitude_trait_base.rds"))

best_model_species<- magnitude_species_pod_pa
best_model_traits<-magnitude_traits_pod_pa

metadata_pod_pa<- readxl::read_excel("S6_Synthesis_model/data/synthesis_data.xlsx") |> 
  dplyr::filter(!dataset %in% c("N67TTP", "N78TTP", "S47TTP")) |> 
  rename(framework = "beta_type",
         feature = "metric_type") |> 
  filter(framework == "Podani", feature == "pa") |> 
  mutate(facet = ifelse(facet == "Taxonomic","Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover")) |> 
  mutate(facet = factor(facet, c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover")))

direction_colors = rev(c("Dissimilarity increase" = "#ff7f32","Similarity increase" = "#9f5cc0") ) 

contrasts_magnitude<-predictions(best_model_species,  by = c("ecosystem_type","direction"),
                                 newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_species$data,
                                                    direction = unique,
                                                    ecosystem_type = unique,
                                                    main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
  posterior_draws() |> 
  mutate(facet="Taxonomic turnover") |> 
  bind_rows(
    predictions(best_model_traits,  by = c("ecosystem_type","direction"),
                newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_traits$data,
                                   direction = unique,
                                   ecosystem_type = unique,
                                   main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
      posterior_draws() |> mutate(facet="Functional turnover")) |> 
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |> 
  rename(Predictor = "ecosystem_type") |> 
  select(draw,group,facet,class,Predictor,direction) |> 
  bind_rows(
    predictions(best_model_species,  by = c("main_land_use_type","direction"),
                newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_species$data,
                                   direction = unique,
                                   ecosystem_type = unique,
                                   main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
      posterior_draws() |> 
      mutate(facet="Taxonomic turnover") |> 
      bind_rows(predictions(best_model_traits,  by = c("main_land_use_type","direction"),
                            newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_traits$data,
                                               direction = unique,
                                               ecosystem_type = unique,
                                               main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
                  posterior_draws() |> mutate(facet="Functional turnover")) |> 
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(Predictor = "main_land_use_type") |> 
      select(draw,group,facet,class,Predictor,direction)
  )


  contrasts_magnitude |> 
  mutate(
    Predictor = case_when(
      Predictor == "Freshwater"  ~ "Freshwater (n=30)",
      Predictor == "Terrestrial" ~ "Terrestrial (n=131)",
      Predictor == "Agriculture" ~ "Agriculture (n=38)",
      Predictor == "Forest"      ~ "Forest (n=56)",
      Predictor == "Urban"       ~ "Urban (n=15)",
      Predictor == "Multiple"    ~ "Multiple (n=52)",
      TRUE                        ~ Predictor
    ),
    Predictor = factor(
      Predictor,
      levels = c(
        "Freshwater (n=30)",
        "Terrestrial (n=131)",
        rev(c(
          "Agriculture (n=38)",
          "Forest (n=56)",
          "Urban (n=15)",
          "Multiple (n=52)"
        ))
      )
    )
  ) |>
  mutate(
    direction = recode(
      direction,
      "Differentiation" = "Dissimilarity increase",
      "Homogenisation"  = "Similarity increase"
    ),
    direction = factor(
      direction,
      levels = rev(c("Dissimilarity increase", "Similarity increase"))
    )
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      str_detect(group, "^hfpmagnitude") ~ "Human Footprint",
      str_detect(group, "^hetmagnitude") ~ "Habitat Heterogeneity"
    )) |> 
  mutate(group = factor(group,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity"))) |> 
    mutate(facet = factor(facet, levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover"))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(y=Predictor,x=draw,colour=direction))+
    ggstats::geom_stripped_rows(
      aes(y = Predictor),
      colour=NA,
      odd  = "white",
      even = "grey90",
      xfrom = -Inf, xto = Inf
    ) +
  stat_pointinterval(position="dodge", .width=c(.8,.89,.95))+
  ggh4x::facet_nested(class~facet+group, scales="free_y",space="free_y")+
  scale_colour_manual(values=direction_colors)+
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.x =  element_text(face="bold",margin=margin(t=5)),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face="bold",angle=270, margin=margin(l=5)),
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12, margin=margin(t=5)),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 10, margin=margin(t=3)),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.width  = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.8, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.x  = unit(0.5, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.y  = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.margin     = margin(t = 5, b = 3),
    legend.box.margin = margin(t = -5),
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(2,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0)
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) + labs(x = "<b>Cumulative fitted turnover</b>",
           colour="Turnover direction",
           )+
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,NA),
                     labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  ) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,3), clip="on")

```

##### Posterior draws

```{r}
#| label: fig-posterior-draws-pod-pa-mag
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the magnitude of community responses to human pressures for species and Functional turnover following Baselga framework using abundance data**. Point intervals of posterior predictive distributions for dataset-level (n = 161) estimates of the magnitude of human pressure on species and Functional turnover. Positive values indicate a stronger effect of human pressure on compositional change, while negative values suggest a weaker effect. Categorical predictors (italicized) are compared to reference categories: *differentiation* for direction, *terrestrial* for ecosystem type, *multiple* for main land-use type, *1 km* for buffer size in human footprint and “0.5km” for buffer size of habitat heterogeneity estimates. Predictors with credible intervals that do not overlap zero are considered significant and are shown in black. Point estimates indicate median values of posterior draws. The linewidths represent 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest density intervals (HDI), with levels of support classified as strong (95% HDI), moderate (89\\% HDI), weak (80\\% HDI), or no support (\\<80\\% HDI). Values represent parameter medians and their HDI ranges."
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 14cm
#| fig-pos: H


# 1. extract and filter
posterior_plot_data_pod_pa <- generate_figure_data(magnitude_species_pod_pa,magnitude_traits_pod_pa, type="magnitude",draw_min=-5,draw_max=5) |> 
  mutate(
    response = case_when(
      str_detect(response, "^hfpmagnitude") ~ "Human Footprint",
      str_detect(response, "^hetmagnitude") ~ "Habitat Heterogeneity"
    )) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity")))

ggplot(posterior_plot_data_pod_pa, aes(x = posterior, y = parameter, colour = significant)) +
stat_pointinterval(.width=c(.8,.89,.95)) +
  geom_text(
    data = ~ .x |>  distinct(across(any_of(c("response","shape", "parameter", "facet"))), .keep_all = TRUE), # Add text annotations from get_support
    aes(y = parameter, x = 4, label = text),
    hjust = 0, size = convert_size(8)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_grid(response ~ facet, space = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Posterior draws", y = NULL) +
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face="bold",angle=90,size=12),
    strip.text.x = element_text(face="bold",size=12),,
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.y        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 10, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 12),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    legend.position    = "none",
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(2,2,2,2, unit="mm"),
    plot.title= element_text(face="bold",size=12)
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-5,10), clip="off")+
  scale_colour_manual(values=rev(c("black","gray60")))
```

##### Random effect estimates

```{r}
#| label: fig-pod-pa-mag-rf
#| fig-cap: "**Taxon-level variation in the magnitude of community turnover under the Podani framework using presence-absence data.** Posterior estimates of random intercepts for major taxonomic groups (microorganisms, plants, invertebrates, vertebrates) from Bayesian models quantifying the magnitude of species and Functional turnover. Values represent deviations from the overall mean magnitude (sum of I-spline coefficients), indicating whether each taxon exhibits stronger or weaker turnover relative to the global average. Points show posterior medians, and intervals represent 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDIs)."
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 8

# Prepare and summarize data
magnitude_species_re_pod_pa <-
magnitude_species_pod_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |> 
    mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) 

magnitude_traits_re_pod_pa <- 
  magnitude_traits_pod_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover") |> 
    mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) 

# Plot
Figure_27a <- magnitude_species_pod_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |> 
    mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) |>  
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = magnitude_species_re_pod_pa,
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-.7, 1), clip = "on") +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  )  +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )


Figure_27b <- magnitude_traits_pod_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet="Functional turnover") |> 
      mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) |>  
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = magnitude_traits_re_pod_pa |> mutate(taxa_coarse =   str_to_title(taxa_coarse)),
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-.5, 1), clip = "on") +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  )   +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )

Figure_27a / Figure_27b
```

### Baselga

#### Abundance

##### Posterior predictions

```{r}
#| label: fig-pred-bas-abun-mag
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the magnitude of human-pressure effects on community turnover following the Baselga framework using abundance data.** Posterior predictions of the magnitude of effect in each direction across ecosystem type (terrestrial, freshwater) and main land-use type (agriculture, forest, urban, multiple-use). For each group, the central dot is the median prediction, and the linewidth represent the 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals; an interval that does not overlap zero is interpreted as a significant tendency toward homogenisation or differentiation (see Methods). The x-axis indicates the weighted effect size, estimated by the sum of human pressure I-splines from BBGDM. Effect sizes were weighted by the proportion of valid bootstraps (target = 1000), penalising models with lower success rates. Dots represent median values, with lines showing 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest density intervals (HDI). Relationships were considered significant if the HDI did not include zero."
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 5.5
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

magnitude_species_bas_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Abundance/magnitude/magnitude_species_base.rds"))

magnitude_traits_bas_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Abundance/magnitude/magnitude_trait_base.rds"))

best_model_species<- magnitude_species_bas_abun
best_model_traits<-magnitude_traits_bas_abun

metadata_bas_abun<- readxl::read_excel("S6_Synthesis_model/data/synthesis_data.xlsx") |> 
  dplyr::filter(!dataset %in% c("N67TTP", "N78TTP", "S47TTP")) |> 
  rename(framework = "beta_type",
         feature = "metric_type") |> 
  filter(framework == "Baselga", feature == "abun") |> 
  mutate(facet = ifelse(facet == "Taxonomic","Species","Traits"))

direction_colors = rev(c("Dissimilarity increase" = "#ff7f32","Similarity increase" = "#9f5cc0") ) 

contrasts_magnitude<-predictions(best_model_species,  by = c("ecosystem_type","direction"),
                                 newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_species$data,
                                                    direction = unique,
                                                    ecosystem_type = unique,
                                                    main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
  posterior_draws() |> 
  mutate(facet="Taxonomic turnover") |> 
  bind_rows(
    predictions(best_model_traits,  by = c("ecosystem_type","direction"),
                newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_traits$data,
                                   direction = unique,
                                   ecosystem_type = unique,
                                   main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
      posterior_draws() |> mutate(facet="Functional turnover")) |> 
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |> 
  rename(Predictor = "ecosystem_type") |> 
  select(draw,group,facet,class,Predictor,direction) |> 
  bind_rows(
    predictions(best_model_species,  by = c("main_land_use_type","direction"),
                newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_species$data,
                                   direction = unique,
                                   ecosystem_type = unique,
                                   main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
      posterior_draws() |> 
      mutate(facet="Taxonomic turnover") |> 
      bind_rows(predictions(best_model_traits,  by = c("main_land_use_type","direction"),
                            newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_traits$data,
                                               direction = unique,
                                               ecosystem_type = unique,
                                               main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
                  posterior_draws() |> mutate(facet="Functional turnover")) |> 
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(Predictor = "main_land_use_type") |> 
      select(draw,group,facet,class,Predictor,direction)
  )


  contrasts_magnitude |> 
  mutate(
    Predictor = case_when(
      Predictor == "Freshwater"  ~ "Freshwater (n=30)",
      Predictor == "Terrestrial" ~ "Terrestrial (n=131)",
      Predictor == "Agriculture" ~ "Agriculture (n=38)",
      Predictor == "Forest"      ~ "Forest (n=56)",
      Predictor == "Urban"       ~ "Urban (n=15)",
      Predictor == "Multiple"    ~ "Multiple (n=52)",
      TRUE                        ~ Predictor
    ),
    Predictor = factor(
      Predictor,
      levels = c(
        "Freshwater (n=30)",
        "Terrestrial (n=131)",
        rev(c(
          "Agriculture (n=38)",
          "Forest (n=56)",
          "Urban (n=15)",
          "Multiple (n=52)"
        ))
      )
    )
  ) |>
  mutate(
    direction = recode(
      direction,
      "Differentiation" = "Dissimilarity increase",
      "Homogenisation"  = "Similarity increase"
    ),
    direction = factor(
      direction,
      levels = rev(c("Dissimilarity increase", "Similarity increase"))
    )
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      str_detect(group, "^hfpmagnitude") ~ "Human Footprint",
      str_detect(group, "^hetmagnitude") ~ "Habitat Heterogeneity"
    )) |> 
  mutate(group = factor(group,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity"))) |> 
    mutate(facet = factor(facet, levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover"))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(y=Predictor,x=draw,colour=direction))+
    ggstats::geom_stripped_rows(
      aes(y = Predictor),
      colour=NA,
      odd  = "white",
      even = "grey90",
      xfrom = -Inf, xto = Inf
    ) +
  stat_pointinterval(position="dodge", .width=c(.8,.89,.95))+
  ggh4x::facet_nested(class~facet+group, scales="free_y",space="free_y")+
  scale_colour_manual(values=direction_colors)+
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.x =  element_text(face="bold",margin=margin(t=5)),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face="bold",angle=270, margin=margin(l=5)),
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12, margin=margin(t=5)),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 10, margin=margin(t=3)),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.width  = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.8, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.x  = unit(0.5, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.y  = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.margin     = margin(t = 5, b = 3),
    legend.box.margin = margin(t = -5),
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(2,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0)
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) + labs(x = "<b>Cumulative fitted turnover</b>",
           colour="Turnover direction",
           )+
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,NA),
                     labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  ) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,3), clip="on")

```

##### Posterior draws

```{r}
#| label: fig-posterior-draws-bas-abun-mag
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 14cm
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the magnitude of community responses to human pressures for species and Functional turnover following Baselga framework using abundance data**. Point intervals of posterior predictive distributions for dataset-level (n = 161) estimates of the magnitude of human pressure on species and Functional turnover. Positive values indicate a stronger effect of human pressure on compositional change, while negative values suggest a weaker effect. Categorical predictors (italicized) are compared to reference categories: *differentiation* for direction, *terrestrial* for ecosystem type, *multiple* for main land-use type, *1 km* for buffer size in human footprint and *0.5km* for buffer size of habitat heterogeneity estimates. Predictors with credible intervals that do not overlap zero are considered significant and are shown in black. Point estimates indicate median values of posterior draws. The linewidths represent 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest density intervals (HDI), with levels of support classified as strong (95% HDI), moderate (89\\% HDI), weak (80\\% HDI), or no support (\\<80\\% HDI). Values represent parameter medians and their HDI ranges."
#| fig-pos: H

# 1. extract and filter
posterior_plot_data_bas_abun <- generate_figure_data(magnitude_species_bas_abun,magnitude_traits_bas_abun, type="magnitude",draw_min=-5,draw_max=5) |> 
  mutate(
    response = case_when(
      str_detect(response, "^hfpmagnitude") ~ "Human Footprint",
      str_detect(response, "^hetmagnitude") ~ "Habitat Heterogeneity"
    )) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity")))

ggplot(posterior_plot_data_bas_abun, aes(x = posterior, y = parameter, colour = significant)) +
stat_pointinterval(.width=c(.8,.89,.95)) +
  geom_text(
    data = ~ .x |>  distinct(across(any_of(c("response","shape", "parameter", "facet"))), .keep_all = TRUE), # Add text annotations from get_support
    aes(y = parameter, x = 4, label = text),
    hjust = 0, size = convert_size(8)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_grid(response ~ facet, space = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Posterior draws", y = NULL) +
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face="bold",angle=90,size=12),
    strip.text.x = element_text(face="bold",size=12),,
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.y        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 10, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 12),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    legend.position    = "none",
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(2,2,2,2, unit="mm"),
    plot.title= element_text(face="bold",size=12)
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-5,10), clip="on")+
  scale_colour_manual(values=rev(c("black","gray60")))
```

##### Random effect estimates

```{r}
#| label: fig-bas-abun-mag-rf
#| fig-cap: "**Taxon-level variation in the magnitude of community turnover under the Baselga framework using abundance data.** Posterior estimates of random intercepts for major taxonomic groups (microorganisms, plants, invertebrates, vertebrates) derived from Bayesian models quantifying the magnitude of species and Functional turnover. Values represent deviations from the overall mean effect size (sum of I-spline coefficients), indicating whether each taxon shows stronger or weaker turnover relative to the global average. Points denote posterior medians, while thick and thin bars correspond to 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDIs)."
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 8


# Prepare and summarize data
magnitude_species_re_bas_abun <-
magnitude_species_bas_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |> 
      mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) 

magnitude_traits_re_bas_abun <- 
  magnitude_traits_bas_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover") |> 
      mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) 

# Plot
Figure_30a <- magnitude_species_bas_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |> 
      mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = magnitude_species_re_bas_abun,
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2, 3), clip = "on") +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),

    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),

    legend.position = "none",
  )   +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )

  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],

  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,

    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  )   +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )

Figure_30a / Figure_30b 
```

#### Presence-absence

##### Posterior predictions

```{r}
#| label: fig-pred-bas-pa-mag
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the magnitude of human-pressure effects on community turnover following the Baselga framework using presence–absence data.** Posterior predictions of the magnitude of effect in each direction across ecosystem type (terrestrial, freshwater) and main land-use type (agriculture, forest, urban, multiple-use). For each group, the central dot is the median prediction, and the linewidth represent the 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals; an interval that does not overlap zero is interpreted as a significant tendency toward homogenisation or differentiation (see Methods). The x-axis indicates the weighted effect size, estimated by the sum of human pressure I-splines from BBGDM. Effect sizes were weighted by the proportion of valid bootstraps (target = 1000), penalising models with lower success rates. Dots represent median values, with lines showing 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest density intervals (HDI). Relationships were considered significant if the HDI did not include zero."
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 5.5
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

magnitude_species_bas_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Presence_absence/magnitude/magnitude_species_base.rds"))

magnitude_traits_bas_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Presence_absence/magnitude/magnitude_trait_base.rds"))

best_model_species<- magnitude_species_bas_pa
best_model_traits<-magnitude_traits_bas_pa

metadata_bas_pa<- readxl::read_excel("S6_Synthesis_model/data/synthesis_data.xlsx") |> 
  dplyr::filter(!dataset %in% c("N67TTP", "N78TTP", "S47TTP")) |> 
  rename(framework = "beta_type",
         feature = "metric_type") |> 
  filter(framework == "Podani", feature == "pa") |> 
  mutate(facet = ifelse(facet == "Taxonomic","Species","Traits"))


direction_colors = rev(c("Dissimilarity increase" = "#ff7f32","Similarity increase" = "#9f5cc0") ) 

contrasts_magnitude<-predictions(best_model_species,  by = c("ecosystem_type","direction"),
                                 newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_species$data,
                                                    direction = unique,
                                                    ecosystem_type = unique,
                                                    main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
  posterior_draws() |> 
  mutate(facet="Taxonomic turnover") |> 
  bind_rows(
    predictions(best_model_traits,  by = c("ecosystem_type","direction"),
                newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_traits$data,
                                   direction = unique,
                                   ecosystem_type = unique,
                                   main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
      posterior_draws() |> mutate(facet="Functional turnover")) |> 
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |> 
  rename(Predictor = "ecosystem_type") |> 
  select(draw,group,facet,class,Predictor,direction) |> 
  bind_rows(
    predictions(best_model_species,  by = c("main_land_use_type","direction"),
                newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_species$data,
                                   direction = unique,
                                   ecosystem_type = unique,
                                   main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
      posterior_draws() |> 
      mutate(facet="Taxonomic turnover") |> 
      bind_rows(predictions(best_model_traits,  by = c("main_land_use_type","direction"),
                            newdata = datagrid(newdata=best_model_traits$data,
                                               direction = unique,
                                               ecosystem_type = unique,
                                               main_land_use_type=unique), re_formula=NA) |> 
                  posterior_draws() |> mutate(facet="Functional turnover")) |> 
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(Predictor = "main_land_use_type") |> 
      select(draw,group,facet,class,Predictor,direction)
  )


  contrasts_magnitude |> 
  mutate(
    Predictor = case_when(
      Predictor == "Freshwater"  ~ "Freshwater (n=30)",
      Predictor == "Terrestrial" ~ "Terrestrial (n=131)",
      Predictor == "Agriculture" ~ "Agriculture (n=38)",
      Predictor == "Forest"      ~ "Forest (n=56)",
      Predictor == "Urban"       ~ "Urban (n=15)",
      Predictor == "Multiple"    ~ "Multiple (n=52)",
      TRUE                        ~ Predictor
    ),
    Predictor = factor(
      Predictor,
      levels = c(
        "Freshwater (n=30)",
        "Terrestrial (n=131)",
        rev(c(
          "Agriculture (n=38)",
          "Forest (n=56)",
          "Urban (n=15)",
          "Multiple (n=52)"
        ))
      )
    )
  ) |>
  mutate(
    direction = recode(
      direction,
      "Differentiation" = "Dissimilarity increase",
      "Homogenisation"  = "Similarity increase"
    ),
    direction = factor(
      direction,
      levels = rev(c("Dissimilarity increase", "Similarity increase"))
    )
  ) |> 
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      str_detect(group, "^hfpmagnitude") ~ "Human Footprint",
      str_detect(group, "^hetmagnitude") ~ "Habitat Heterogeneity"
    )) |> 
  mutate(group = factor(group,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity"))) |> 
    mutate(facet = factor(facet, levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover"))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(y=Predictor,x=draw,colour=direction))+
    ggstats::geom_stripped_rows(
      aes(y = Predictor),
      colour=NA,
      odd  = "white",
      even = "grey90",
      xfrom = -Inf, xto = Inf
    ) +
  stat_pointinterval(position="dodge", .width=c(.8,.89,.95))+
  ggh4x::facet_nested(class~facet+group, scales="free_y",space="free_y")+
  scale_colour_manual(values=direction_colors)+
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.x =  element_text(face="bold",margin=margin(t=5)),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face="bold",angle=270, margin=margin(l=5)),
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12, margin=margin(t=5)),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 10, margin=margin(t=3)),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.width  = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.8, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.x  = unit(0.5, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.y  = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.margin     = margin(t = 5, b = 3),
    legend.box.margin = margin(t = -5),
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(2,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0)
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) + labs(x = "<b>Cumulative fitted turnover</b>",
           colour="Turnover direction",
           )+
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,NA),
                     labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  ) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,3), clip="on")

```

##### Posterior draws

```{r}
#| label: fig-posterior-draws-bas-pa-mag
#| fig-width: 10.5
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 14cm
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the magnitude of community responses to human pressures for species and Functional turnover following Baselga framework using presence-absence data**. Point intervals of posterior predictive distributions for dataset-level (n = 161) estimates of the magnitude of human pressure on species and Functional turnover. Positive values indicate a stronger effect of human pressure on compositional change, while negative values suggest a weaker effect. Categorical predictors (italicized) are compared to reference categories: *differentiation* for direction, *terrestrial* for ecosystem type, *multiple* for main land-use type, *1 km* for buffer size in human footprint and *0.5km* for buffer size of habitat heterogeneity estimates. Predictors with credible intervals that do not overlap zero are considered significant and are shown in black. Point estimates indicate median values of posterior draws. The linewidths represent 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest density intervals (HDI), with levels of support classified as strong (95% HDI), moderate (89\\% HDI), weak (80\\% HDI), or no support (\\<80\\% HDI). Values represent parameter medians and their HDI ranges."
#| fig-pos: H

# 1. extract and filter
posterior_plot_data_bas_pa <- generate_figure_data(magnitude_species_bas_pa,magnitude_traits_bas_pa, type="magnitude",draw_min=-5,draw_max=5) |> 
  mutate(
    response = case_when(
      str_detect(response, "^hfpmagnitude") ~ "Human Footprint",
      str_detect(response, "^hetmagnitude") ~ "Habitat Heterogeneity"
    )) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity")))

ggplot(posterior_plot_data_bas_pa, aes(x = posterior, y = parameter, colour = significant)) +
stat_pointinterval(.width=c(.8,.89,.95)) +
  geom_text(
    data = ~ .x |>  distinct(across(any_of(c("response","shape", "parameter", "facet"))), .keep_all = TRUE), # Add text annotations from get_support
    aes(y = parameter, x = 4, label = text),
    hjust = 0, size = convert_size(8)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_grid(response ~ facet, space = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Posterior draws", y = NULL) +
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face="bold",angle=90,size=12),
    strip.text.x = element_text(face="bold",size=12),,
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.y        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 10, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 12),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    legend.position    = "none",
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(2,2,2,2, unit="mm"),
    plot.title= element_text(face="bold",size=12)
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-5,10), clip="off")+
  scale_colour_manual(values=rev(c("black","gray60")))
```

##### Random effect estimates

```{r}
#| label: fig-bas-pa-mag-rf
#| fig-cap: "**Taxon-level variation in the magnitude of community turnover under the Baselga framework using presence-absence data.** Posterior estimates of random intercepts for major taxonomic groups (microorganisms, plants, invertebrates, vertebrates) derived from Bayesian models quantifying the magnitude of species and Functional turnover. Values represent deviations from the overall mean effect size (sum of I-spline coefficients), indicating whether each taxon shows stronger or weaker turnover relative to the global average. Points denote posterior medians, while thick and thin bars correspond to 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDIs)."
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 8

magnitude_species_re_bas_pa <-
magnitude_species_bas_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
mutate(label = glue::glue("{formatC(value, format = 'f', digits = 2)} [{formatC(.lower, format = 'f', digits = 2)}, {formatC(.upper, format = 'f', digits = 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |> 
      mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) 

magnitude_traits_re_bas_pa <- 
  magnitude_traits_bas_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
mutate(label = glue::glue("{formatC(value, format = 'f', digits = 2)} [{formatC(.lower, format = 'f', digits = 2)}, {formatC(.upper, format = 'f', digits = 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |>
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover") |> 
      mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) 

# Plot
Figure_33a <- magnitude_species_bas_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
     r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Human_Footprint = r_taxa_coarse__hfpmagnitude,
         Habitat_Heterogeneity = r_taxa_coarse__hetmagnitude) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Human_Footprint","Habitat_Heterogeneity"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |> 
      mutate(predictor = gsub("_", " ", predictor)) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
```

## Response shape

### Podani

#### Abundance
##### Posterior predictions 

```{r}
#| label: fig-pred-pod-abun-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the shape of community turnover along the human-pressure gradient following the Podani framework using presence–absence data.** (a) Schematic representation of the four characteristic turnover-response shapes—saturating, exponential, reverse logistic, and absent—derived from coefficients of Bayesian bootstrap generalised dissimilarity models (BBGDM; see Methods). The number of datasets exhibiting each predominant shape is indicated above each illustration. (b) Cumulative distribution functions of posterior probabilities for dataset-level variation in turnover shape across species and Functional turnover, showing the probability of occurrence for each response type across ecosystem and main land-use categories. The x-axis indicates the probability of observing each shape, based on 999 bootstrap iterations included in a Bayesian multinomial model across all 161 datasets. Points represent posterior medians, with linewidths showing 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI). CCDF bars display the full posterior probability distribution, where a sharp decay indicates high certainty (posterior mass concentrated near the median), and a gradual decay indicates greater uncertainty or skewness. Relationships were considered significant when the HDI did not include zero."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 7.7
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

shape_hfp_species_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_hfp_species.rds"))
shape_hfp_traits_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_hfp_traits.rds"))

shape_het_species_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_het_species.rds"))
shape_het_traits_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_het_traits.rds"))

direction_colors = c("Differentiation" = "#ff7f32","Homogenisation" = "#9f5cc0")  

contrasts_shape_hfp_pod_abun <- shape_hfp_species_pod_abun |>
  add_epred_draws(
    newdata = datagrid(
      newdata = shape_hfp_species_pod_abun$data,
      direction = unique,
      ecosystem_type = unique,
      main_land_use_type = unique,
      hfp_trials = 1
    ),
    re_formula = NA,
    ndraws = 1000
  ) |>
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_hfp_traits_pod_abun |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_hfp_traits_pod_abun$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          hfp_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
  ) |>
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |>
  rename(
    Predictor = ecosystem_type,
    draw = .draw,
    estimate = .epred,
    group = .category
  ) |>
  select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate) |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_hfp_species_pod_abun |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_hfp_species_pod_abun$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          hfp_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
      bind_rows(
        shape_hfp_traits_pod_abun |>
          add_epred_draws(
            newdata = datagrid(
              newdata = shape_hfp_traits_pod_abun$data,
              direction = unique,
              ecosystem_type = unique,
              main_land_use_type = unique,
              hfp_trials = 1
            ),
            re_formula = NA,
            ndraws = 1000
          ) |>
          mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
      ) |>
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(
        Predictor = main_land_use_type,
        draw = .draw,
        estimate = .epred,
        group = .category
      ) |>
      select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate)
  ) |>
  mutate(response = "Human Footprint")


contrasts_shape_hh_pod_abun <- shape_het_species_pod_abun |>
  add_epred_draws(
    newdata = datagrid(
      newdata = shape_het_species_pod_abun$data,
      direction = unique,
      ecosystem_type = unique,
      main_land_use_type = unique,
      het_trials = 1
    ),
    re_formula = NA,
    ndraws = 1000
  ) |>
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_het_traits_pod_abun |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_het_traits_pod_abun$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          het_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
  ) |>
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |>
  rename(
    Predictor = ecosystem_type,
    draw = .draw,
    estimate = .epred,
    group = .category
  ) |>
  select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate) |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_het_species_pod_abun |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_het_species_pod_abun$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          het_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
      bind_rows(
        shape_het_traits_pod_abun |>
          add_epred_draws(
            newdata = datagrid(
              newdata = shape_het_traits_pod_abun$data,
              direction = unique,
              ecosystem_type = unique,
              main_land_use_type = unique,
              het_trials = 1
            ),
            re_formula = NA,
            ndraws = 1000
          ) |>
          mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
      ) |>
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(
        Predictor = main_land_use_type,
        draw = .draw,
        estimate = .epred,
        group = .category
      ) |>
      select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate)
  ) |>
  mutate(response = "Habitat Heterogeneity")


contrasts_shape_pod_abun <- bind_rows(contrasts_shape_hfp_pod_abun, contrasts_shape_hh_pod_abun) |>
  mutate(response = factor(response, levels = c("Human Footprint", "Habitat Heterogeneity")))


contrasts_shape_pod_abun |> 
  mutate(group = str_to_sentence(group)) |> 
  mutate(group = gsub("Revlog","Reverse logistic",group)) |> 
  mutate(
    Predictor = case_when(
      Predictor == "Freshwater"  ~ "Freshwater (n=30)",
      Predictor == "Terrestrial" ~ "Terrestrial (n=131)",
      Predictor == "Agriculture" ~ "Agriculture (n=38)",
      Predictor == "Forest"      ~ "Forest (n=56)",
      Predictor == "Urban"       ~ "Urban (n=15)",
      Predictor == "Multiple"    ~ "Multiple (n=52)",
      TRUE                        ~ Predictor
    ),
    Predictor = factor(
      Predictor,
      levels = c(
        "Freshwater (n=30)",
        "Terrestrial (n=131)",
        rev(c(
          "Agriculture (n=38)",
          "Forest (n=56)",
          "Urban (n=15)",
          "Multiple (n=52)"
        ))
      )
    )
  ) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = factor(facet,levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(y=group,x=estimate,colour=direction, fill=direction))+
  geom_stripped_rows(
    aes(y = group),
    colour=NA,
    odd  = "white",
    even = "grey90",
    xfrom = -Inf, xto = Inf
  ) +
  stat_ccdfinterval(position="dodge", .width=c(.8,.89,.95), slab_alpha=.5) +
  ggh4x::facet_nested(class+Predictor~facet+response, scales="free_y",space="free_y",
                      # use strip_themed() instead of strip_nested()
                      strip = strip_nested(
                        # supply one size for the "class" layer, one for the "Predictor" layer
                        text_y      = elem_list_text(size = c(10, 8)),
                        by_layer_y = TRUE,
                        text_x      = elem_list_text(size = c(10, 8)),
                        by_layer_x = TRUE,
                      ))+
scale_colour_manual(
  values = direction_colors,
  breaks = c("Homogenisation", "Differentiation"),
  labels = c("Increasing similarity", "Increasing dissimilarity")
) +
scale_fill_manual(
  values = direction_colors,
  breaks = c("Homogenisation", "Differentiation"),
  labels = c("Increasing similarity", "Increasing dissimilarity")
)+
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.x =  element_text(face="bold",margin=margin(t=5)),
    strip.text.y = element_text(size=10,face="bold",angle=270, margin=margin(l=5)),
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12, margin=margin(t=5)),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 8, margin=margin(t=3)),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(2,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0),
    legend.position="bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face="bold")
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) + labs(x = "Posterior predictions (Probability of observing a shape)",
           colour="Relationship direction",
           fill="Relationship direction")+
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,NA),
                     labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  )

```

##### Random effect estimates

```{r}
#| label: fig-pod-abun-rf-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Taxon-specific deviations in the response shape of community turnover following the Podani framework using abundance data.** Posterior estimates of random effects for coarse taxonomic groups showing deviations in the probability of exponential, reverse-logistic, and saturating turnover responses under human footprint and habitat heterogeneity gradients. Estimates represent deviations from the global mean probability of each response shape, indicating whether specific taxa are more or less likely to exhibit a given turnover shape Points denote posterior medians, and linewidths show 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI). All estimates are expressed on the random-effect scale derived from Bayesian multinomial models."
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-width: 16
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

shape_hfp_species_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Abundance/shape/model_base_hfp_species.rds"))
shape_hfp_traits_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Abundance/shape/model_base_hfp_traits.rds"))

shape_het_species_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Abundance/shape/model_base_het_species.rds"))
shape_het_traits_pod_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Abundance/shape/model_base_het_traits.rds"))

# Prepare and summarize data
shape_hfp_species_re_pod_abun<-
shape_hfp_species_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Human Footprint")

shape_hfp_traits_re_pod_abun<-
shape_hfp_traits_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover", variable = "Human Footprint")

shape_het_species_re_pod_abun<-
shape_het_species_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Habitat Heterogeneity")

shape_het_traits_re_pod_abun<-
shape_het_traits_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
    mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover", variable = "Habitat Heterogeneity")

# Plot
Figure_34a <- 
shape_hfp_species_pod_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
    mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Human Footprint") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = shape_hfp_species_re_pod_abun,
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(variable~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(l=5), angle=270),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  )  +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )
```
