```{r}
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(l=5), angle=270),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  )  +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )

Figure_34a / Figure_34b / Figure_34c / Figure_34d
```

#### Presence-absence

##### Posterior predictions

```{r}
#| label: fig-pred-pod-pa-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the shape of community turnover along the human-pressure gradient following the Podani framework using presence–absence data.** (a) Schematic representation of the four characteristic turnover-response shapes—saturating, exponential, reverse logistic, and absent—derived from coefficients of Bayesian bootstrap generalised dissimilarity models (BBGDM; see Methods). The number of datasets exhibiting each predominant shape is indicated above each illustration. (b) Cumulative distribution functions of posterior probabilities for dataset-level variation in turnover shape across species and Functional turnover, showing the probability of occurrence for each response type across ecosystem and main land-use categories. The x-axis indicates the probability of observing each shape, based on 999 bootstrap iterations included in a Bayesian multinomial model across all 161 datasets. Points represent posterior medians, with linewidths showing 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI). CCDF bars display the full posterior probability distribution, where a sharp decay indicates high certainty (posterior mass concentrated near the median), and a gradual decay indicates greater uncertainty or skewness. Relationships were considered significant when the HDI did not include zero."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 7.7
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H
shape_hfp_species_pod_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_hfp_species.rds"))
shape_hfp_traits_pod_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_hfp_traits.rds"))

shape_het_species_pod_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_het_species.rds"))
shape_het_traits_pod_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Podani/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_het_traits.rds"))


direction_colors = c("Differentiation" = "#ff7f32","Homogenisation" = "#9f5cc0")  


contrasts_shape_hfp_pod_pa <- shape_hfp_species_pod_pa |>
  add_epred_draws(
    newdata = datagrid(
      newdata = shape_hfp_species_pod_pa$data,
      direction = unique,
      ecosystem_type = unique,
      main_land_use_type = unique,
      hfp_trials = 1
    ),
    re_formula = NA,
    ndraws = 1000
  ) |>
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_hfp_traits_pod_pa |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_hfp_traits_pod_pa$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          hfp_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
  ) |>
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |>
  rename(
    Predictor = ecosystem_type,
    draw = .draw,
    estimate = .epred,
    group = .category
  ) |>
  select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate) |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_hfp_species_pod_pa |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_hfp_species_pod_pa$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          hfp_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
      bind_rows(
        shape_hfp_traits_pod_pa |>
          add_epred_draws(
            newdata = datagrid(
              newdata = shape_hfp_traits_pod_pa$data,
              direction = unique,
              ecosystem_type = unique,
              main_land_use_type = unique,
              hfp_trials = 1
            ),
            re_formula = NA,
            ndraws = 1000
          ) |>
          mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
      ) |>
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(
        Predictor = main_land_use_type,
        draw = .draw,
        estimate = .epred,
        group = .category
      ) |>
      select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate)
  ) |>
  mutate(response = "Human Footprint")

contrasts_shape_hh_pod_pa <- shape_het_species_pod_pa |>
  add_epred_draws(
    newdata = datagrid(
      newdata = shape_het_species_pod_pa$data,
      direction = unique,
      ecosystem_type = unique,
      main_land_use_type = unique,
      het_trials = 1
    ),
    re_formula = NA,
    ndraws = 1000
  ) |>
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_het_traits_pod_pa |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_het_traits_pod_pa$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          het_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
  ) |>
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |>
  rename(
    Predictor = ecosystem_type,
    draw = .draw,
    estimate = .epred,
    group = .category
  ) |>
  select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate) |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_het_species_pod_pa |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_het_species_pod_pa$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          het_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
      bind_rows(
        shape_het_traits_pod_pa |>
          add_epred_draws(
            newdata = datagrid(
              newdata = shape_het_traits_pod_pa$data,
              direction = unique,
              ecosystem_type = unique,
              main_land_use_type = unique,
              het_trials = 1
            ),
            re_formula = NA,
            ndraws = 1000
          ) |>
          mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
      ) |>
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(
        Predictor = main_land_use_type,
        draw = .draw,
        estimate = .epred,
        group = .category
      ) |>
      select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate)
  ) |>
  mutate(response = "Habitat Heterogeneity")


contrasts_shape_pod_pa <- bind_rows(contrasts_shape_hfp_pod_pa, contrasts_shape_hh_pod_pa) |>
  mutate(response = factor(response, levels = c(
    "Human Footprint", "Habitat Heterogeneity"
  )))


contrasts_shape_pod_pa |> 
  mutate(group = str_to_sentence(group)) |> 
  mutate(group = gsub("Revlog","Reverse logistic",group)) |> 
  mutate(
    Predictor = case_when(
      Predictor == "Freshwater"  ~ "Freshwater (n=30)",
      Predictor == "Terrestrial" ~ "Terrestrial (n=131)",
      Predictor == "Agriculture" ~ "Agriculture (n=38)",
      Predictor == "Forest"      ~ "Forest (n=56)",
      Predictor == "Urban"       ~ "Urban (n=15)",
      Predictor == "Multiple"    ~ "Multiple (n=52)",
      TRUE                        ~ Predictor
    ),
    Predictor = factor(
      Predictor,
      levels = c(
        "Freshwater (n=30)",
        "Terrestrial (n=131)",
        rev(c(
          "Agriculture (n=38)",
          "Forest (n=56)",
          "Urban (n=15)",
          "Multiple (n=52)"
        ))
      )
    )
  ) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity"))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(y=group,x=estimate,colour=direction, fill=direction))+
  geom_stripped_rows(
    aes(y = group),
    colour=NA,
    odd  = "white",
    even = "grey90",
    xfrom = -Inf, xto = Inf
  ) +
  stat_ccdfinterval(position="dodge", .width=c(.8,.89,.95), slab_alpha=.5) +
  ggh4x::facet_nested(class+Predictor~facet+response, scales="free_y",space="free_y",
                      # use strip_themed() instead of strip_nested()
                      strip = strip_nested(
                        # supply one size for the "class" layer, one for the "Predictor" layer
                        text_y      = elem_list_text(size = c(10, 8)),
                        by_layer_y = TRUE,
                        text_x      = elem_list_text(size = c(10, 8)),
                        by_layer_x = TRUE,
                      ))+
scale_colour_manual(
  values = direction_colors,
  breaks = c("Homogenisation", "Differentiation"),
  labels = c("Increasing similarity", "Increasing dissimilarity")
) +
scale_fill_manual(
  values = direction_colors,
  breaks = c("Homogenisation", "Differentiation"),
  labels = c("Increasing similarity", "Increasing dissimilarity")
)+
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.x =  element_text(face="bold",margin=margin(t=5)),
    strip.text.y = element_text(size=10,face="bold",angle=270, margin=margin(l=5)),
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12, margin=margin(t=5)),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 8, margin=margin(t=3)),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(2,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0),
    legend.position="bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face="bold")
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) + labs(x = "Posterior predictions (Probability of observing a shape)",
           colour="Relationship direction",
           fill="Relationship direction")+
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,NA),
                     labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  )
```

##### Posterior draws

```{r}
#| label:  fig-posterior-draws-pod-pa-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the shape of community turnover along the human-pressure gradient following the Podani framework using presence–absence data.** Point intervals of posterior predictive distributions for dataset-level (n = 160) estimates of the shape of species and Functional turnover along the human pressure gradient. Positive values indicate a higher probability of observing a given turnover shape, while negative values indicate a lower probability. Categorical predictors (italicized) are compared to reference categories: *differentiation* for direction, *terrestrial* for ecosystem type, *multiple* for main land-use type, *1 km* for buffer size in human footprint and *0.5km* for buffer size of habitat heterogeneity estimates. Predictors with credible intervals that do not overlap zero are considered significant. Colour shading indicates 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI), with levels of support classified as strong (95\\% HDI), moderate (89\\% HDI), weak (80\\% HDI), or no support (\\<80\\% HDI). Values represent posterior medians and their HDI ranges."
#| fig-height: 15
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-width: 15
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

posterior_plot_data_HFP_pod_pa <- generate_figure_data(shape_hfp_species_pod_pa,shape_hfp_traits_pod_pa, type="shape",draw_min=-5,draw_max=5) |> 
  mutate(response = "Human Footprint")  

posterior_plot_data_HH_pod_pa <- generate_figure_data(shape_het_species_pod_pa,shape_het_traits_pod_pa, type="shape",draw_min=-5,draw_max=5) |> 
  mutate(response = "Habitat Heterogeneity") 

posterior_plot_data_pod_pa<- bind_rows(posterior_plot_data_HFP_pod_pa,posterior_plot_data_HH_pod_pa) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity"))) |> 
  mutate(shape = str_to_sentence(shape))

# 1. Tag each row as either a draw or a text annotation
draws_df <- posterior_plot_data_pod_pa |>
  mutate(layer = " ")

text_df <- posterior_plot_data_pod_pa |>
  distinct(shape,parameter, facet, response, text, draw_min, draw_max) |>
  # position text slightly beyond the max draw
  mutate(posterior = draw_max * 1.05,
         layer     = "  ")

plot_df_pod_pa <- bind_rows(draws_df, text_df) |>
  mutate(layer = factor(layer, levels = c(" ", "  "))) 

  ggplot(plot_df_pod_pa) +
  facet_nested(
    facet+response~shape+layer,
    scales      = "free_x",
    space       = "free_x",
  )+
  stat_pointinterval(
    data    = ~ .x |>  filter(layer == " "),
    aes(x = posterior, y = parameter, colour = significant),
    .width = c(.8, .89, .95)
  ) +
  # text labels
  geom_text(
    data    =~ .x |> filter(layer == "  "),
    aes(x = posterior, y = parameter, label = text),
    hjust   = 0,
    size    = convert_size(5)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  # nested facets: rows = Posterior/Text, cols = your facet (Species vs Trait)
  # per‐strip x scales
  facetted_pos_scales(
    x = list(
      # for the Posterior strip
      layer == " " ~ scale_x_continuous(
        name   = "Posterior draws",
        expand = expansion(mult = 0.02)
      ),
      # for the Text strip
      layer == "  " ~ scale_x_continuous(
        limits = c(5.2,7),
        breaks=NULL,
        name   = NULL,
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
      )
    )
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("gray60", "black")) +
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face="bold",angle=270,size=12),
    strip.text.x = element_text(face="bold",size=12),,
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.y        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 10, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 12),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    legend.position    = "none",
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(2,2,2,2, unit="mm"),
    plot.title= element_text(face="bold",size=12)
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) 
```

##### Random effect estimates

```{r}
#| label: fig-pod-pa-rf-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Taxon-specific deviations in the response shape of community turnover following the Podani framework using presence-absence data.** Posterior estimates of random effects for coarse taxonomic groups showing deviations in the probability of exponential, reverse-logistic, and saturating turnover responses under human footprint and habitat heterogeneity gradients. Estimates represent deviations from the global mean probability of each response shape, indicating whether specific taxa are more or less likely to exhibit a given turnover shape Points denote posterior medians, and linewidths show 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI). All estimates are expressed on the random-effect scale derived from Bayesian multinomial models."
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-width: 16
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

shape_hfp_species_re_pod_pa<-
  shape_hfp_species_pod_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Human Footprint")

shape_hfp_traits_re_pod_pa<-
  shape_hfp_traits_pod_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover", variable = "Human Footprint")

shape_het_species_re_pod_pa<-
  shape_het_species_pod_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Habitat Heterogeneity")

shape_het_traits_re_pod_pa<-
  shape_het_traits_pod_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover", variable = "Habitat Heterogeneity")

# Plot
Figure_37a <- 
  shape_hfp_species_pod_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Human Footprint") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = shape_hfp_species_re_pod_pa,
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(variable~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(l=5), angle=270),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  )  +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )

Figure_37b <- 
  shape_het_species_pod_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Habitat Heterogeneity") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = shape_het_species_re_pod_pa,
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(variable~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(l=5), angle=270),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  )  +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )
```

### Baselga

#### Abundance

##### Posterior predictions

```{r}
#| label: fig-pred-bas-abun-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the shape of community turnover along the human-pressure gradient following the Baselga framework using abundance data.** (a) Schematic representation of the four characteristic turnover-response shapes—saturating, exponential, reverse logistic, and absent—derived from coefficients of Bayesian bootstrap generalised dissimilarity models (BBGDM; see Methods). The number of datasets exhibiting each predominant shape is indicated above each illustration. (b) Cumulative distribution functions of posterior probabilities for dataset-level variation in turnover shape across species and Functional turnover, showing the probability of occurrence for each response type across ecosystem and main land-use categories. The x-axis indicates the probability of observing each shape, based on 999 bootstrap iterations included in a Bayesian multinomial model across all 161 datasets. Points represent posterior medians, with linewidths showing 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI). CCDF bars display the full posterior probability distribution, where a sharp decay indicates high certainty (posterior mass concentrated near the median), and a gradual decay indicates greater uncertainty or skewness. Relationships were considered significant when the HDI did not include zero."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 7.7
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H 
shape_hfp_species_bas_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Abundance/shape/model_base_hfp_species.rds"))
shape_hfp_traits_bas_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Abundance/shape/model_base_hfp_traits.rds"))

shape_het_species_bas_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Abundance/shape/model_base_het_species.rds"))
shape_het_traits_bas_abun<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Abundance/shape/model_base_het_traits.rds"))


ecosystem_type_colors<- c("Freshwater" = "#118ab2","Terrestrial"= "#06d6a0")
direction_colors = c("Differentiation" = "#ff7f32","Homogenisation" = "#9f5cc0")  
land_use_colors<-c("Agriculture"="#a36627","Forest"="#848c04","Urban"="#1c1c0c","Multiple"="#dc7c5c")

contrasts_shape_hfp_bas_abun <- shape_hfp_species_bas_abun |>
  add_epred_draws(newdata=datagrid(newdata = shape_hfp_species_bas_abun$data,
                           direction = unique, ecosystem_type = unique,
                           main_land_use_type = unique, hfp_trials = 1),
                  re_formula = NA, ndraws = 1000) |>
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_hfp_traits_bas_abun |>
      add_epred_draws(newdata=datagrid(newdata = shape_hfp_traits_bas_abun$data,
                               direction = unique, ecosystem_type = unique,
                               main_land_use_type = unique, hfp_trials = 1),
                      re_formula = NA, ndraws = 1000) |>
      mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
  ) |>
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |>
  rename(Predictor = ecosystem_type, draw = .draw, estimate = .epred, group = .category) |>
  select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate) |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_hfp_species_bas_abun |>
      add_epred_draws(newdata=datagrid(newdata = shape_hfp_species_bas_abun$data,
                               direction = unique, ecosystem_type = unique,
                               main_land_use_type = unique, hfp_trials = 1),
                      re_formula = NA, ndraws = 1000) |>
      mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
      bind_rows(
        shape_hfp_traits_bas_abun |>
          add_epred_draws(newdata=datagrid(newdata = shape_hfp_traits_bas_abun$data,
                                   direction = unique, ecosystem_type = unique,
                                   main_land_use_type = unique, hfp_trials = 1),
                          re_formula = NA, ndraws = 1000) |>
          mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
      ) |>
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(Predictor = main_land_use_type, draw = .draw, estimate = .epred, group = .category) |>
      select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate)
  ) |>
  mutate(response = "Human Footprint")


contrasts_shape_hh_bas_abun <- shape_het_species_bas_abun |>
  add_epred_draws(newdata=datagrid(newdata = shape_het_species_bas_abun$data,
                           direction = unique, ecosystem_type = unique,
                           main_land_use_type = unique, het_trials = 1),
                  re_formula = NA, ndraws = 1000) |>
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_het_traits_bas_abun |>
      add_epred_draws(newdata=datagrid(newdata = shape_het_traits_bas_abun$data,
                               direction = unique, ecosystem_type = unique,
                               main_land_use_type = unique, het_trials = 1),
                      re_formula = NA, ndraws = 1000) |>
      mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
  ) |>
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |>
  rename(Predictor = ecosystem_type, draw = .draw, estimate = .epred, group = .category) |>
  select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate) |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_het_species_bas_abun |>
      add_epred_draws(newdata=datagrid(newdata = shape_het_species_bas_abun$data,
                               direction = unique, ecosystem_type = unique,
                               main_land_use_type = unique, het_trials = 1),
                      re_formula = NA, ndraws = 1000) |>
      mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
      bind_rows(
        shape_het_traits_bas_abun |>
          add_epred_draws(newdata=datagrid(newdata = shape_het_traits_bas_abun$data,
                                   direction = unique, ecosystem_type = unique,
                                   main_land_use_type = unique, het_trials = 1),
                          re_formula = NA, ndraws = 1000) |>
          mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
      ) |>
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(Predictor = main_land_use_type, draw = .draw, estimate = .epred, group = .category) |>
      select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate)
  ) |>
  mutate(response = "Habitat Heterogeneity")



contrasts_shape_bas_abun <- bind_rows(contrasts_shape_hfp_bas_abun,contrasts_shape_hh_bas_abun) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity")))


contrasts_shape_bas_abun |> 
  mutate(group = str_to_sentence(group)) |> 
  mutate(group = gsub("Revlog","Reverse logistic",group)) |> 
  mutate(
    Predictor = case_when(
      Predictor == "Freshwater"  ~ "Freshwater (n=30)",
      Predictor == "Terrestrial" ~ "Terrestrial (n=131)",
      Predictor == "Agriculture" ~ "Agriculture (n=38)",
      Predictor == "Forest"      ~ "Forest (n=56)",
      Predictor == "Urban"       ~ "Urban (n=15)",
      Predictor == "Multiple"    ~ "Multiple (n=52)",
      TRUE                        ~ Predictor
    ),
    Predictor = factor(
      Predictor,
      levels = c(
        "Freshwater (n=30)",
        "Terrestrial (n=131)",
        rev(c(
          "Agriculture (n=38)",
          "Forest (n=56)",
          "Urban (n=15)",
          "Multiple (n=52)"
        ))
      )
    )
  ) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity"))) |> 
    mutate(facet = factor(facet,levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(y=group,x=estimate,colour=direction, fill=direction))+
  geom_stripped_rows(
    aes(y = group),
    colour=NA,
    odd  = "white",
    even = "grey90",
    xfrom = -Inf, xto = Inf
  ) +
  stat_ccdfinterval(position="dodge", .width=c(.8,.89,.95), slab_alpha=.5) +
  ggh4x::facet_nested(class+Predictor~facet+response, scales="free_y",space="free_y",
                      # use strip_themed() instead of strip_nested()
                      strip = strip_nested(
                        # supply one size for the "class" layer, one for the "Predictor" layer
                        text_y      = elem_list_text(size = c(10, 8)),
                        by_layer_y = TRUE,
                        text_x      = elem_list_text(size = c(10, 8)),
                        by_layer_x = TRUE,
                      ))+
scale_colour_manual(
  values = direction_colors,
  breaks = c("Homogenisation", "Differentiation"),
  labels = c("Increasing similarity", "Increasing dissimilarity")
) +
scale_fill_manual(
  values = direction_colors,
  breaks = c("Homogenisation", "Differentiation"),
  labels = c("Increasing similarity", "Increasing dissimilarity")
)+
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.x =  element_text(face="bold",margin=margin(t=5)),
    strip.text.y = element_text(size=10,face="bold",angle=270, margin=margin(l=5)),
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12, margin=margin(t=5)),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 8, margin=margin(t=3)),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(2,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0),
    legend.position="bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face="bold")
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) + labs(x = "Posterior predictions (Probability of observing a shape)",
           colour="Relationship direction",
           fill="Relationship direction")+
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,NA),
                     labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  )

```

##### Posterior draws

```{r}
#| label:  fig-posterior-draws-bas-abun-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the shape of community turnover along the human-pressure gradient following the Baselga framework using abundance data.** Point intervals of posterior predictive distributions for dataset-level (n = 160) estimates of the shape of species and Functional turnover along the human pressure gradient. Positive values indicate a higher probability of observing a given turnover shape, while negative values indicate a lower probability. Categorical predictors (italicized) are compared to reference categories: *differentiation* for direction, *terrestrial* for ecosystem type, *multiple* for main land-use type, *1 km* for buffer size in human footprint and *0.5km* for buffer size of habitat heterogeneity estimates. Predictors with credible intervals that do not overlap zero are considered significant. Colour shading indicates 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI), with levels of support classified as strong (95\\% HDI), moderate (89\\% HDI), weak (80\\% HDI), or no support (\\<80\\% HDI). Values represent posterior medians and their HDI ranges."
#| fig-height: 15
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-width: 15
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H
#| 
posterior_plot_data_HFP_bas_abun <- generate_figure_data(shape_hfp_species_bas_abun,shape_hfp_traits_bas_abun, type="shape",draw_min=-5,draw_max=5) |> 
  mutate(response = "Human Footprint")  

posterior_plot_data_HH_bas_abun <- generate_figure_data(shape_het_species_bas_abun,shape_het_traits_bas_abun, type="shape",draw_min=-5,draw_max=5) |> 
  mutate(response = "Habitat Heterogeneity") 

posterior_plot_data_bas_abun<- bind_rows(posterior_plot_data_HFP_bas_abun,posterior_plot_data_HH_bas_abun) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity"))) |> 
  mutate(shape = str_to_sentence(shape))

# 1. Tag each row as either a draw or a text annotation
draws_df <- posterior_plot_data_bas_abun |>
  mutate(layer = " ")

text_df <- posterior_plot_data_bas_abun |>
  distinct(shape,parameter, facet, response, text, draw_min, draw_max) |>
  # position text slightly beyond the max draw
  mutate(posterior = draw_max * 1.05,
         layer     = "  ")

plot_df_bas_abun <- bind_rows(draws_df, text_df) |>
  mutate(layer = factor(layer, levels = c(" ", "  "))) 


  ggplot(plot_df_bas_abun) +
  facet_nested(
    facet+response~shape+layer,
    scales      = "free_x",
    space       = "free_x",
  )+
  stat_pointinterval(
    data    = ~ .x |>  filter(layer == " "),
    aes(x = posterior, y = parameter, colour = significant),
    .width = c(.8, .89, .95)
  ) +
  # text labels
  geom_text(
    data    =~ .x |> filter(layer == "  "),
    aes(x = posterior, y = parameter, label = text),
    hjust   = 0,
    size    = convert_size(5)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  # nested facets: rows = Posterior/Text, cols = your facet (Species vs Trait)
  # per‐strip x scales
  facetted_pos_scales(
    x = list(
      # for the Posterior strip
      layer == " " ~ scale_x_continuous(
        name   = "Posterior draws",
        expand = expansion(mult = 0.02)
      ),
      # for the Text strip
      layer == "  " ~ scale_x_continuous(
        limits = c(5.2,7),
        breaks=NULL,
        name   = NULL,
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
      )
    )
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("gray60", "black")) +
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face="bold",angle=270,size=12),
    strip.text.x = element_text(face="bold",size=12),,
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.y        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 10, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 12),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    legend.position    = "none",
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(2,2,2,2, unit="mm"),
    plot.title= element_text(face="bold",size=12)
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) 
```

##### Random effect estimates

```{r}
#| label: fig-bas-abun-rf-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Taxon-specific deviations in the response shape of community turnover following the Baselga framework using abundance data.** Posterior estimates of random effects for coarse taxonomic groups showing deviations in the probability of exponential, reverse-logistic, and saturating turnover responses under human footprint and habitat heterogeneity gradients. Estimates represent deviations from the global mean probability of each response shape, indicating whether specific taxa are more or less likely to exhibit a given turnover shape Points denote posterior medians, and linewidths show 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI). All estimates are expressed on the random-effect scale derived from Bayesian multinomial models."
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-width: 16
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

#Prepare and summarize data

shape_hfp_species_re_bas_abun<-
  shape_hfp_species_bas_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Human Footprint")

shape_hfp_traits_re_bas_abun<-
  shape_hfp_traits_bas_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover", variable = "Human Footprint")

shape_het_species_re_bas_abun<-
  shape_het_species_bas_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Habitat Heterogeneity")

shape_het_traits_re_bas_abun<-
  shape_het_traits_bas_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover", variable = "Habitat Heterogeneity")

# Plot
Figure_40a <- 
  shape_hfp_species_bas_abun |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Human Footprint") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = shape_hfp_species_re_bas_abun,
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(variable~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(l=5), angle=270),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  )  +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )
```

#### Presence-absence

##### Posterior predictions

```{r}
#| label: fig-pred-bas-pa-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the shape of community turnover along the human-pressure gradient following the Podani framework using presence–absence data.** (a) Schematic representation of the four characteristic turnover-response shapes—saturating, exponential, reverse logistic, and absent—derived from coefficients of Bayesian bootstrap generalised dissimilarity models (BBGDM; see Methods). The number of datasets exhibiting each predominant shape is indicated above each illustration. (b) Cumulative distribution functions of posterior probabilities for dataset-level variation in turnover shape across species and Functional turnover, showing the probability of occurrence for each response type across ecosystem and main land-use categories. The x-axis indicates the probability of observing each shape, based on 999 bootstrap iterations included in a Bayesian multinomial model across all 161 datasets. Points represent posterior medians, with linewidths showing 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI). CCDF bars display the full posterior probability distribution, where a sharp decay indicates high certainty (posterior mass concentrated near the median), and a gradual decay indicates greater uncertainty or skewness. Relationships were considered significant when the HDI did not include zero."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 7.7
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

shape_hfp_species_bas_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_hfp_species.rds"))
shape_hfp_traits_bas_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_hfp_traits.rds"))

shape_het_species_bas_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_het_species.rds"))
shape_het_traits_bas_pa<- read_rds(here::here("S7_Model_outputs_figures_and_tables/model/Baselga/Presence_absence/shape/model_base_het_traits.rds"))


ecosystem_type_colors<- c("Freshwater" = "#118ab2","Terrestrial"= "#06d6a0")
direction_colors = c("Differentiation" = "#ff7f32","Homogenisation" = "#9f5cc0")  
land_use_colors<-c("Agriculture"="#a36627","Forest"="#848c04","Urban"="#1c1c0c","Multiple"="#dc7c5c")

contrasts_shape_hfp_bas_pa <- shape_hfp_species_bas_pa |>
  add_epred_draws(
    newdata = datagrid(
      newdata = shape_hfp_species_bas_pa$data,
      direction = unique,
      ecosystem_type = unique,
      main_land_use_type = unique,
      hfp_trials = 1
    ),
    re_formula = NA,
    ndraws = 1000
  ) |>
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_hfp_traits_bas_pa |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_hfp_traits_bas_pa$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          hfp_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
  ) |>
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |>
  rename(
    Predictor = ecosystem_type,
    draw = .draw,
    estimate = .epred,
    group = .category
  ) |>
  select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate) |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_hfp_species_bas_pa |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_hfp_species_bas_pa$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          hfp_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
      bind_rows(
        shape_hfp_traits_bas_pa |>
          add_epred_draws(
            newdata = datagrid(
              newdata = shape_hfp_traits_bas_pa$data,
              direction = unique,
              ecosystem_type = unique,
              main_land_use_type = unique,
              hfp_trials = 1
            ),
            re_formula = NA,
            ndraws = 1000
          ) |>
          mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
      ) |>
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(
        Predictor = main_land_use_type,
        draw = .draw,
        estimate = .epred,
        group = .category
      ) |>
      select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate)
  ) |>
  mutate(response = "Human Footprint")


contrasts_shape_hh_bas_pa <- shape_het_species_bas_pa |>
  add_epred_draws(
    newdata = datagrid(
      newdata = shape_het_species_bas_pa$data,
      direction = unique,
      ecosystem_type = unique,
      main_land_use_type = unique,
      het_trials = 1
    ),
    re_formula = NA,
    ndraws = 1000
  ) |>
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_het_traits_bas_pa |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_het_traits_bas_pa$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          het_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
  ) |>
  mutate(class = "Ecosystem type") |>
  rename(
    Predictor = ecosystem_type,
    draw = .draw,
    estimate = .epred,
    group = .category
  ) |>
  select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate) |>
  bind_rows(
    shape_het_species_bas_pa |>
      add_epred_draws(
        newdata = datagrid(
          newdata = shape_het_species_bas_pa$data,
          direction = unique,
          ecosystem_type = unique,
          main_land_use_type = unique,
          het_trials = 1
        ),
        re_formula = NA,
        ndraws = 1000
      ) |>
      mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover") |>
      bind_rows(
        shape_het_traits_bas_pa |>
          add_epred_draws(
            newdata = datagrid(
              newdata = shape_het_traits_bas_pa$data,
              direction = unique,
              ecosystem_type = unique,
              main_land_use_type = unique,
              het_trials = 1
            ),
            re_formula = NA,
            ndraws = 1000
          ) |>
          mutate(facet = "Functional turnover")
      ) |>
      mutate(class = "Main land use type") |>
      rename(
        Predictor = main_land_use_type,
        draw = .draw,
        estimate = .epred,
        group = .category
      ) |>
      select(draw, group, facet, class, Predictor, direction, estimate)
  ) |>
  mutate(response = "Habitat Heterogeneity")


contrasts_shape_bas_pa <- bind_rows(contrasts_shape_hfp_bas_pa, contrasts_shape_hh_bas_pa) |>
  mutate(response = factor(response, levels = c("Human Footprint", "Habitat Heterogeneity")))


contrasts_shape_bas_pa |> 
  mutate(group = str_to_sentence(group)) |> 
  mutate(group = gsub("Revlog","Reverse logistic",group)) |> 
  mutate(
    Predictor = case_when(
      Predictor == "Freshwater"  ~ "Freshwater (n=30)",
      Predictor == "Terrestrial" ~ "Terrestrial (n=131)",
      Predictor == "Agriculture" ~ "Agriculture (n=38)",
      Predictor == "Forest"      ~ "Forest (n=56)",
      Predictor == "Urban"       ~ "Urban (n=15)",
      Predictor == "Multiple"    ~ "Multiple (n=52)",
      TRUE                        ~ Predictor
    ),
    Predictor = factor(
      Predictor,
      levels = c(
        "Freshwater (n=30)",
        "Terrestrial (n=131)",
        rev(c(
          "Agriculture (n=38)",
          "Forest (n=56)",
          "Urban (n=15)",
          "Multiple (n=52)"
        ))
      )
    )
  ) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity"))) |>
    mutate(facet = factor(facet,levels=c("Taxonomic turnover","Functional turnover"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(y=group,x=estimate,colour=direction, fill=direction))+
  geom_stripped_rows(
    aes(y = group),
    colour=NA,
    odd  = "white",
    even = "grey90",
    xfrom = -Inf, xto = Inf
  ) +
  stat_ccdfinterval(position="dodge", .width=c(.8,.89,.95), slab_alpha=.5) +
  ggh4x::facet_nested(class+Predictor~facet+response, scales="free_y",space="free_y",
                      # use strip_themed() instead of strip_nested()
                      strip = strip_nested(
                        # supply one size for the "class" layer, one for the "Predictor" layer
                        text_y      = elem_list_text(size = c(10, 8)),
                        by_layer_y = TRUE,
                        text_x      = elem_list_text(size = c(10, 8)),
                        by_layer_x = TRUE,
                      ))+
scale_colour_manual(
  values = direction_colors,
  breaks = c("Homogenisation", "Differentiation"),
  labels = c("Increasing similarity", "Increasing dissimilarity")
) +
scale_fill_manual(
  values = direction_colors,
  breaks = c("Homogenisation", "Differentiation"),
  labels = c("Increasing similarity", "Increasing dissimilarity")
)+
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.x =  element_text(face="bold",margin=margin(t=5)),
    strip.text.y = element_text(size=10,face="bold",angle=270, margin=margin(l=5)),
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12, margin=margin(t=5)),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 8, margin=margin(t=3)),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(2,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0),
    legend.position="bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face="bold")
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) + labs(x = "Posterior predictions (Probability of observing a shape)",
           colour="Relationship direction",
           fill="Relationship direction")+
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,NA),
                     labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  )

```

##### Posterior draws

```{r}
#| label:  fig-posterior-draws-bas-pa-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Dataset-level variation in the shape of community turnover along the human-pressure gradient following the Baselga framework using presence–absence data.** Point intervals of posterior predictive distributions for dataset-level (n = 160) estimates of the shape of species and Functional turnover along the human pressure gradient. Positive values indicate a higher probability of observing a given turnover shape, while negative values indicate a lower probability. Categorical predictors (italicized) are compared to reference categories: *differentiation* for direction, *terrestrial* for ecosystem type, *multiple* for main land-use type, *1 km* for buffer size in human footprint and *0.5km* for buffer size of habitat heterogeneity estimates. Predictors with credible intervals that do not overlap zero are considered significant. Colour shading indicates 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI), with levels of support classified as strong (95\\% HDI), moderate (89\\% HDI), weak (80\\% HDI), or no support (\\<80\\% HDI). Values represent posterior medians and their HDI ranges."
#| fig-height: 15
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-width: 15
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H


posterior_plot_data_HFP_bas_pa <- generate_figure_data(shape_hfp_species_bas_pa,shape_hfp_traits_bas_pa, type="shape",draw_min=-5,draw_max=5) |> 
  mutate(response = "Human Footprint")  

posterior_plot_data_HH_bas_pa <- generate_figure_data(shape_het_species_bas_pa,shape_het_traits_bas_pa, type="shape",draw_min=-5,draw_max=5) |> 
  mutate(response = "Habitat Heterogeneity") 

posterior_plot_data_bas_pa<- bind_rows(posterior_plot_data_HFP_bas_pa,posterior_plot_data_HH_bas_pa) |> 
  mutate(response = factor(response,levels=c("Human Footprint","Habitat Heterogeneity"))) |> 
  mutate(shape = str_to_sentence(shape))

# 1. Tag each row as either a draw or a text annotation
draws_df <- posterior_plot_data_bas_pa |>
  mutate(layer = " ")

text_df <- posterior_plot_data_bas_pa |>
  distinct(shape,parameter, facet, response, text, draw_min, draw_max) |>
  # position text slightly beyond the max draw
  mutate(posterior = draw_max * 1.05,
         layer     = "  ")

plot_df_bas_pa <- bind_rows(draws_df, text_df) |>
  mutate(layer = factor(layer, levels = c(" ", "  "))) 

  ggplot(plot_df_bas_pa) +
  facet_nested(
    facet+response~shape+layer,
    scales      = "free_x"
  )+
  stat_pointinterval(
    data    = ~ .x |>  filter(layer == " "),
    aes(x = posterior, y = parameter, colour = significant),
    .width = c(.8, .89, .95)
  ) +
  # text labels
  geom_text(
    data    =~ .x |> filter(layer == "  "),
    aes(x = posterior, y = parameter, label = text),
    hjust   = 0,
    size    = convert_size(8)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  # nested facets: rows = Posterior/Text, cols = your facet (Species vs Trait)
  # per‐strip x scales
  facetted_pos_scales(
    x = list(
      # for the Posterior strip
      layer == " " ~ scale_x_continuous(
        name   = "Posterior draws"
      ),
      # for the Text strip
      layer == "  " ~ scale_x_continuous(
        limits = c(5.2,7),
        breaks=NULL,
        name   = NULL,
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
      )
    )
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("gray60", "black")) +
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_blank(),
    panel.background =  element_blank(),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face="bold",angle=270,size=12),
    strip.text.x = element_text(face="bold",size=12),,
    axis.title.x       = ggtext::element_markdown(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.y        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 10, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x        = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 12),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    axis.line.x        = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x       = element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    legend.position    = "none",
    plot.tag           = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0,"lines"),
    plot.margin = margin(2,2,2,2, unit="mm"),
    plot.title= element_text(face="bold",size=12)
  ) +
  guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  ) 

```

##### Random effect estimates

```{r}
#| label: fig-bas-pa-rf-shp
#| fig-cap: "**Taxon-specific deviations in the response shape of community turnover following the Baselga framework using presence-absence data.** Posterior estimates of random effects for coarse taxonomic groups showing deviations in the probability of exponential, reverse-logistic, and saturating turnover responses under human footprint and habitat heterogeneity gradients. Estimates represent deviations from the global mean probability of each response shape, indicating whether specific taxa are more or less likely to exhibit a given turnover shape Points denote posterior medians, and linewidths show 80\\%, 89\\%, and 95\\% highest-density intervals (HDI). All estimates are expressed on the random-effect scale derived from Bayesian multinomial models."
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-width: 16
#| out-width: 15cm
#| fig-pos: H

shape_hfp_species_re_bas_pa<-
  shape_hfp_species_bas_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Human Footprint")

shape_hfp_traits_re_bas_pa<-
  shape_hfp_traits_bas_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  group_by(taxa_coarse,predictor) |>
  median_qi(value, .width = 0.95) |>
  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover", variable = "Human Footprint")

shape_het_species_re_bas_pa<-
  shape_het_species_bas_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,

  mutate(label = glue::glue("{round(value, 2)} [{round(.lower, 2)}, {round(.upper, 2)}]")) |>

  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |>

shape_het_traits_re_bas_pa<-

    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],

# Plot
Figure_43a <- 
  shape_hfp_species_bas_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Taxonomic turnover", variable = "Human Footprint") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = shape_hfp_species_re_bas_pa,
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(variable~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
#  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1.5, 3), clip = "on") +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(l=5), angle=270),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  ) +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )
```
