```{r}
    strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(l=5), angle=270),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  ) +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )

Figure_43d <- 
  shape_het_traits_bas_pa |>
  spread_draws(
    r_taxa_coarse__muexponential[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__musaturating[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
    r_taxa_coarse__murevlog[taxa_coarse, Intercept],
  ) |> 
  rename(Exponential = r_taxa_coarse__muexponential,
         Saturating = r_taxa_coarse__musaturating,
         Reverse_logistic = r_taxa_coarse__murevlog) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Exponential","Saturating","Reverse_logistic"), names_to = "predictor",values_to = "value") |> 
  mutate(taxa_coarse = stringr::str_to_title(taxa_coarse)) |>
  mutate(taxa_coarse = factor(taxa_coarse, levels=c("Microorganism","Plant","Invertebrate","Vertebrate"))) |> 
  mutate(facet = "Functional turnover", variable = "Habitat Heterogeneity") |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(taxa_coarse))) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(.8, .89, .95), fill = "gray70", slab_color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0,linetype=3)+
  geom_text(data = shape_het_traits_re_bas_pa,
            aes(label = label, x = .upper + .05),
            hjust = 0, vjust=-1, size = 3.5) +
  facet_nested(variable~facet+predictor)+
  labs(x = "Posterior estimate (taxon-specific deviation)", y = NULL) +
  ggplot2::theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(b=5)),
    strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold",size=12,margin = margin(l=5), angle=270),
    axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",hjust = 0.5,margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(family = "sans",face = "bold",angle = 90,margin = margin(r = 4)),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30",margin = margin(t = 5)),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(family = "sans",color = "grey30"),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.x = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    axis.line.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.1),
    axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    plot.margin =  margin(3, 3, 3, 3),
    plot.tag = element_text(face="bold",size=14),
    legend.position = "none",
  )  +   guides(
    x = guide_axis(cap = TRUE),
    y = guide_axis(cap = TRUE)
  )

Figure_43a/Figure_43b/Figure_43c/Figure_43d
```

## Null models

### Direction of relationship

```{r}
#| label: tbl-dir-null
#| tbl-cap: "**Summary of null-model deviations in the direction of trait-based community turnover.** Each cell reports the percentage of datasets in which the observed direction of community turnover—estimated from Harrell–Davis Δr² values—deviates from null expectations (|SES| > 1.96). The null model preserves the observed species composition while randomising trait–species associations, testing whether functional differentiation or homogenisation explains more variation than expected by chance. Within each direction, columns show the proportion of datasets with weaker (W), random (R), or stronger (S) probability of the observed direction relative to the null, together with sample size (n). Bold values indicate the dominant outcome (highest of W, R, or S) within each direction. Results are summarised across dissimilarity frameworks (Baselga and Podani) and data types (abundance and presence–absence)."
#| tbl-pos: H

null_data <- readxl::read_excel(here::here("S5_Null_models/null_data_filtered.xlsx"))

ses_threshold <- 1.96

summary_tbl <- null_data |>
  ungroup() |> 
  filter(iter > 99) |>
  mutate(
    effect_direction = case_when(
      direction_SES >  ses_threshold ~ "Stronger than expected",
      direction_SES < -ses_threshold ~ "Weaker than expected",
      TRUE                           ~ "Random"
    )
  ) |>
  count(framework, data_type, direction, effect_direction, name = "n") |>
  group_by(framework, data_type, direction) |>
  mutate(
    n_total = sum(n),
    pct = 100 * n / n_total
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(framework, data_type, direction, n_total, effect_direction, pct) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = effect_direction,
    values_from = pct,
    values_fill = 0
  ) |>
  mutate(
    # Capitalize for display
    Framework = str_to_title(framework),
    Direction = str_to_title(direction),
    Data_type = case_when(
      data_type == "abun" ~ "Abundance",
      data_type == "pa"   ~ "Presence-absence",
      TRUE ~ str_to_title(data_type)
    ),
    n = as.integer(n_total)
  ) |>
  select(Framework, Data_type, Direction, n,
         `Stronger than expected`, `Weaker than expected`, Random) |>
  # Reorder so Podani comes first
  mutate(Framework = factor(Framework, levels = c("Podani", "Baselga"))) |>
  arrange(Framework, Data_type, Direction)




summary_tbl |>
  mutate(
    Framework = str_to_title(Framework),
    Data_type = case_when(
      Data_type == "abun" ~ "Abundance",
      Data_type == "pa" ~ "Presence–absence",
      TRUE ~ str_to_title(Data_type)
    ),
    Direction = str_to_title(Direction)
  ) |>
  arrange(Framework, Data_type, Direction) |>
  relocate(Framework, Data_type) |>
  mutate(
    n = as.integer(n),
    `Stronger than expected` = round(`Stronger than expected`, 1),
    `Weaker than expected`   = round(`Weaker than expected`, 1),
    Random                   = round(Random, 1)
  ) |>
  select(
    Framework, Data_type, Direction, n,
    `Weaker than expected`, Random, `Stronger than expected`
  ) |>
group_by(Framework, Data_type, Direction) |>
  mutate(
    max_col = pmax(`Weaker than expected`, Random, `Stronger than expected`),
    `Weaker than expected` = ifelse(`Weaker than expected` == max_col,
                                    paste0("\\textbf{", `Weaker than expected`, "}"),
                                    as.character(`Weaker than expected`)),
    Random = ifelse(Random == max_col,
                    paste0("\\textbf{", Random, "}"),
                    as.character(Random)),
    `Stronger than expected` = ifelse(`Stronger than expected` == max_col,
                                      paste0("\\textbf{", `Stronger than expected`, "}"),
                                      as.character(`Stronger than expected`))
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(-max_col) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Direction,
    values_from = c(`Weaker than expected`, Random, `Stronger than expected`, n),
    names_glue = "{Direction}_{.value}"
  ) |>
  select(Framework,Data_type,contains("Differentiation"),contains("Homogenisation")) |> 
  kbl(
    format   = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align    = c("l", "l", rep("c", 8)),
    col.names = c(
      "Framework", "Data type",
      "W (\\%)", "R (\\%)", "S (\\%)", "n",
      "W (\\%)", "R (\\%)", "S (\\%)", "n"
    ),
    escape = FALSE,
    linesep = ""
  ) |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE, html_font = "Helvetica") |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1:2, valign = "middle") |>
  add_header_above(
    c(" " = 2,
      "Differentiation" = 4,
      "Homogenisation" = 4),
    bold = TRUE, line = TRUE
  ) |>
  add_header_above(
    c(" " = 2, "Direction of trait-based turnover" = 8),
    bold = TRUE, line = TRUE
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position"),
    font_size = 9,
    stripe_color = "#f9f9f9"
  )


```

### Magnitude of relationship

```{r}
#| label: tbl-mag-null
#| tbl-cap: "**Summary of null-model deviations in the magnitude of trait-based turnover.** Each cell reports the percentage of datasets in which the observed trait response magnitude—calculated as the sum of I-spline coefficients—deviates from null expectations (|SES| > 1.96) after holding species compositional change constant. Values are grouped by dissimilarity framework (Baselga or Podani), data type (abundance or presence–absence), and environmental gradient (human footprint or habitat heterogeneity). Within each turnover direction—differentiation or homogenisation—columns show the proportion of datasets with weaker (W), random (R), or stronger (S) trait responses than expected. Bold values indicate the dominant outcome within each direction. These results quantify how much functional turnover departs from what would arise purely from the observed taxonomic replacement."
#| tbl-pos: H


# significance threshold for SES (two-tailed ≈ 0.05)
ses_threshold <- 1.96

# --- Prepare long-format summary for HFP and HET ----
magnitude_summary <- null_data |>
  filter(iter > 99) |>
  select(
    dataset, framework, data_type, direction,
    magnitude_hfp_ses, magnitude_het_ses
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(magnitude_hfp_ses, magnitude_het_ses),
    names_to = "gradient",
    values_to = "SES"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    gradient = recode(gradient,
                      "magnitude_hfp_ses" = "Human footprint",
                      "magnitude_het_ses" = "Habitat heterogeneity"),
    effect_direction = case_when(
      SES >  ses_threshold ~ "Stronger than expected",
      SES < -ses_threshold ~ "Weaker than expected",
      TRUE                 ~ "Random"
    )
  ) |>
  group_by(framework, data_type, direction, gradient, effect_direction) |>
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(framework, data_type, direction, gradient) |>
  mutate(pct = 100 * n / sum(n)) |>
  mutate(n=sum(n)) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = effect_direction,
    values_from = pct,
    values_fill = 0
  ) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 1))

# --- Build table ----

# Prepare wide table with Direction spanners (Diff: W/R/S; Hom: W/R/S)
table_S5_wide <- magnitude_summary %>%
  mutate(
    Framework = str_to_title(framework),
    Data_type = case_when(
      data_type == "abun" ~ "Abundance",
      data_type == "pa"   ~ "Presence–absence",
      TRUE ~ str_to_title(data_type)
    ),
    Direction = str_to_title(direction)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Framework, Data_type, gradient, Direction) %>%
  select(
    Framework, Data_type, gradient, Direction,
    Weaker = `Weaker than expected`,
    Random  = Random,
    Stronger = `Stronger than expected`
  ) %>%
  rename(Gradient = gradient) %>%
  # Long → Wide: columns like Diff_W, Diff_R, Diff_S, Hom_W, Hom_R, Hom_S
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Weaker, Random, Stronger),
    names_to = "Cat", values_to = "Val"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Cat = recode(Cat, Weaker = "W", Random = "R", Stronger = "S"),
    Direction = recode(Direction, Differentiation = "Diff", Homogenisation = "Hom")
  ) %>%
  unite("DirCat", Direction, Cat, sep = "_") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = DirCat, values_from = Val) %>%
  # Ensure all needed columns exist even if some combos are missing
  mutate(
    Diff_W = coalesce(Diff_W, NA_real_),
    Diff_R = coalesce(Diff_R, NA_real_),
    Diff_S = coalesce(Diff_S, NA_real_),
    Hom_W  = coalesce(Hom_W , NA_real_),
    Hom_R  = coalesce(Hom_R , NA_real_),
    Hom_S  = coalesce(Hom_S , NA_real_)
  ) %>%
  # Round and format with bold on the max per direction (W/R/S)
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    # Differentiation triplet
    .diff_max = suppressWarnings(max(c(Diff_W, Diff_R, Diff_S), na.rm = TRUE)),
    Diff_W = if (is.na(Diff_W)) "–" else if (Diff_W == .diff_max) paste0("\\textbf{", round(Diff_W, 1), "}") else as.character(round(Diff_W, 1)),
    Diff_R = if (is.na(Diff_R)) "–" else if (Diff_R == .diff_max) paste0("\\textbf{", round(Diff_R, 1), "}") else as.character(round(Diff_R, 1)),
    Diff_S = if (is.na(Diff_S)) "–" else if (Diff_S == .diff_max) paste0("\\textbf{", round(Diff_S, 1), "}") else as.character(round(Diff_S, 1)),
    # Homogenisation triplet
    .hom_max = suppressWarnings(max(c(Hom_W, Hom_R, Hom_S), na.rm = TRUE)),
    Hom_W = if (is.na(Hom_W)) "–" else if (Hom_W == .hom_max) paste0("\\textbf{", round(Hom_W, 1), "}") else as.character(round(Hom_W, 1)),
    Hom_R = if (is.na(Hom_R)) "–" else if (Hom_R == .hom_max) paste0("\\textbf{", round(Hom_R, 1), "}") else as.character(round(Hom_R, 1)),
    Hom_S = if (is.na(Hom_S)) "–" else if (Hom_S == .hom_max) paste0("\\textbf{", round(Hom_S, 1), "}") else as.character(round(Hom_S, 1))
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(
    Framework, Data_type, Gradient,
    Diff_W, Diff_R, Diff_S,
    Hom_W,  Hom_R,  Hom_S
  )

# Build the table with grouped headers and collapsed row groups
kbl(
  format = "latex",
  table_S5_wide,
  booktabs   = TRUE,
  align      = c("l","l","l","c","c","c","c","c","c"),
  escape     = FALSE,
  linesep    = "",
  col.names  = c(
    "Framework", "Data type", "Gradient",
    "W (\\%)", "R (\\%)", "S (\\%)",
    "W (\\%)", "R (\\%)", "S (\\%)"
  )
) |>
  add_header_above(
    c(" " = 3, "Differentiation" = 3, "Homogenisation" = 3),
    bold = TRUE, line = TRUE
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"),
    font_size     = 8
  ) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1:3, valign = "top")

```

### Shape of the relationship

```{r}
#| label: tbl-shp-null
#| tbl-cap: "**Summary of null-model deviations in the shape of trait-based turnover relationships.** Each cell reports the percentage of datasets in which the most frequently observed response shape—saturating, exponential, reverse logistic, or absent—deviates from null expectations (|SES| > 1.96). The null model preserves species composition while randomising trait–species associations, thereby testing whether the dominant shape of the β-diversity response to environmental gradients occurs more or less often than expected by chance. Columns show the proportion of datasets with weaker (W), random (R), or stronger (S) deviations relative to the null, together with the number of datasets (n) exhibiting each modal shape. Results are grouped by dissimilarity framework (Baselga or Podani), data type (abundance or presence–absence), and gradient (human footprint or habitat heterogeneity)."
#| tbl-pos: H

# Define all possible shapes to ensure completion

all_shapes <- c("Saturating", "Exponential", "Reverse logistic", "Absent")

shape_summary <-
  null_data |>
  filter(iter > 99) |>
  mutate(
    Framework = str_to_title(framework),
    Data_type = case_when(
      data_type == "abun" ~ "Abundance",
      data_type == "pa"   ~ "Presence–absence",
      TRUE ~ str_to_title(data_type)
    )
  ) |>
  # Gather both gradients into long form for unified processing
  select(dataset, Framework, Data_type, direction,
         hfp_modal_shape, het_modal_shape,
         hfp_shape_ses, het_shape_ses) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(hfp_modal_shape, het_modal_shape, hfp_shape_ses, het_shape_ses),
    names_to = c("Gradient", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(hfp|het)_(.*)"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Gradient = recode(Gradient,
                      hfp = "Human footprint",
                      het = "Habitat heterogeneity"),
    Shape = str_to_title(modal_shape),
    Category = case_when(
      shape_ses > 1.96  ~ "Stronger",
      shape_ses < -1.96 ~ "Weaker",
      TRUE              ~ "Random"
    )
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(Shape), !is.na(Category)) |>
  group_by(Framework, Data_type, Gradient, Shape, Category, direction) |>
  summarise(
    n_datasets = n_distinct(dataset),
    .groups = "drop_last"
  ) |>
  mutate(prop = 100 * n_datasets / sum(n_datasets), Shape = as.factor(Shape)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(Framework, Data_type, direction, Gradient, Shape, Category, prop, n_datasets) |>
  group_by(Framework, Data_type, Gradient, direction, Shape) |>
  mutate(
    n = max(n_datasets)  # total datasets with that modal shape
  ) |>
  select(-n_datasets) |>
  ungroup() |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Category,
    values_from = prop,
    values_fill = 0
  ) |>
  mutate(across(c(Weaker, Random, Stronger), ~ round(.x, 1)))

# --- Build LaTeX-ready table ---
shape_summary |>
  group_by(Framework, Data_type, direction, Gradient, Shape) |>
  mutate(
    max_val = max(c(Weaker, Random, Stronger), na.rm = TRUE),
    # handle rounding uncertainty (~0.1%) so equal values are both bolded
    Weaker = ifelse(abs(Weaker - max_val) < 0.11,
                    paste0("\\textbf{", sprintf("%.1f", Weaker), "}"),
                    sprintf("%.1f", Weaker)),
    Random = ifelse(abs(Random - max_val) < 0.11,
                    paste0("\\textbf{", sprintf("%.1f", Random), "}"),
                    sprintf("%.1f", Random)),
    Stronger = ifelse(abs(Stronger - max_val) < 0.11,
                      paste0("\\textbf{", sprintf("%.1f", Stronger), "}"),
                      sprintf("%.1f", Stronger)),
    n = as.character(n)
  ) |>
  select(-max_val) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = direction,
    values_from = c(Weaker, Random, Stronger, n),
    names_glue = "{direction}_{.value}",
    values_fill = "0"
  ) |>
  arrange(Framework, Data_type, Gradient, factor(Shape, levels = all_shapes)) |>
  select(Framework,Data_type, Gradient, Shape, contains("homogenisation"),contains("differentiation")) |> 
  kbl(
    format="latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = c("l", "l", "l", rep("c", 9)),
    escape = FALSE,
    linesep = "",
    col.names = c(
      "Framework", "Data type", "Gradient", "Shape",
      "W (\\%)", "R (\\%)", "S (\\%)", "n",
      "W (\\%)", "R (\\%)", "S (\\%)", "n"
    )
  ) |>
  add_header_above(
    c(" " = 4,
      "Homogenisation" = 4,
      "Differentiation" = 4),
    bold = TRUE, line = TRUE
  ) |>
  add_header_above(
    c(" " = 4, "Shape of trait-based turnover relationship" = 8),
    bold = TRUE, line = TRUE
  ) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1:3, valign = "middle") |>
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"),
    font_size = 9,
    stripe_color = "#f9f9f9"
  )
```

## Correlation between predictors

```{r}
#| label: tbl-corr-bbgdm-pred 
#| tbl-cap: "**Dataset-level correlation between human pressure variables included in the Bayesian Bootstrap Generalised Dissimilarity Model**. The table shows the mean pearson correlation alongside the 95\\% confidence interval for the 161 datasets included in the study"
#| tbl-pos: H 

dataset_features<-read.csv(here::here("S1_Preprocessing/Miscellaneous/dataset_features.csv"))
predictor_buffers<-read.csv(here::here("S1_Preprocessing/Miscellaneous/dataset_selected_buffer.csv")) |> 
  select(-var_name)

# Step 2: Pivot dataset_features to long format for matching
long_features <- dataset_features |>
  select(dataset,site,hfp_1000:het_2000) |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = matches("^(hfp|modis|het)_\\d+$"),
               names_to = "var_name", values_to = "value") |> 
  separate(var_name, into=c("predictor","buffer")) |> 
  mutate(buffer = as.numeric(buffer))

# Step 3: Join the buffers to the corresponding site-level values
combined <- predictor_buffers |>
  left_join(long_features, relationship = "many-to-many")

# Step 4: Pivot to wide format so each row has hfp, modis, het as separate columns
final_data <- combined |>
  select(dataset, site, predictor, value,facet) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = predictor, values_from = value ) 


# 1. Compute all three correlations within each dataset × facet
corr_by_group <- final_data |>
  drop_na(hfp, modis, het) |>
  group_by(dataset, facet) |>
  summarise(
    test_hfp_modis = list(cor.test(hfp,      modis)),
    test_hfp_het   = list(cor.test(hfp,      het)),
    test_modis_het = list(cor.test(modis,    het)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    hfp_modis_r   = map_dbl(test_hfp_modis, ~ .x$estimate),
    hfp_modis_lo  = map_dbl(test_hfp_modis, ~ .x$conf.int[1]),
    hfp_modis_hi  = map_dbl(test_hfp_modis, ~ .x$conf.int[2]),
    hfp_het_r     = map_dbl(test_hfp_het,   ~ .x$estimate),
    hfp_het_lo    = map_dbl(test_hfp_het,   ~ .x$conf.int[1]),
    hfp_het_hi    = map_dbl(test_hfp_het,   ~ .x$conf.int[2]),
    modis_het_r   = map_dbl(test_modis_het, ~ .x$estimate),
    modis_het_lo  = map_dbl(test_modis_het, ~ .x$conf.int[1]),
    modis_het_hi  = map_dbl(test_modis_het, ~ .x$conf.int[2])
  ) |>
  select(dataset, facet,
         hfp_modis_r:hfp_modis_hi,
         hfp_het_r:hfp_het_hi,
         modis_het_r:modis_het_hi)

# 2. Pivot into long format so each row is one “pair” + its r, lo, hi
corr_long <- corr_by_group |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(dataset, facet),
    names_to = c("pair", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)_(r|lo|hi)"
  )

# 1. Starting from corr_long (with columns: dataset, facet, pair, r, lo, hi)
summary_table <- corr_long |>
  group_by(facet, pair) |>
  summarise(
    mean_r = mean(r, na.rm = TRUE),
    lo     = mean(lo, na.rm = TRUE),
    hi     = mean(hi, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    `Mean r` = round(mean_r, 2),
    `95% CI` = sprintf("[%.2f, %.2f]", lo, hi)
  ) |>
  separate(pair, into = c("Predictor A", "Predictor B"), sep = "_") |>
  select(facet, `Predictor A`, `Predictor B`, `Mean r`, `95% CI`)

  # 2. Make a single gt table with row‐groups
  summary_table |>
  mutate(
    `Predictor A` = recode(`Predictor A`,
                           hfp   = "Human Footprint",
                           het   = "Habitat Heterogeneity",
                           modis = "MODIS"),
    `Predictor B` = recode(`Predictor B`,
                           hfp   = "Human Footprint",
                           het   = "Habitat Heterogeneity",
                           modis = "MODIS")
  ) |> 
    gt(groupname_col = "facet") |>
    cols_label(
      facet       = "",
      `Predictor A` = "Variable A",
      `Predictor B` = "Variable B"
    ) |>
    opt_row_striping() |> 
    as_latex()
```
